2316: 
2317: def run_live_retrieval(
2318:     *,
2319:     research_question: str,
2320:     amplified_queries: Optional[list[str]] = None,
2321:     protocol: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
2322:     max_serper: int = DEFAULT_MAX_SERPER,
2323:     max_s2: int = DEFAULT_MAX_S2,
2324:     fetch_cap: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_CAP,
2325:     enable_openalex_enrich: bool = True,
2326:     enable_prefetch_filter: bool = False,
2327:     domain: Optional[str] = None,
2328:     seed_urls: Optional[list[str]] = None,
2329:     seed_only: bool = False,
2330:     seed_source: str = "primary_trial_doi",
2331:     seed_query_origin: str = "primary_trial_doi_seed",
2332:     research_frame: Any = None,
2333:     anchor_seed: bool = True,
2334: ) -> LiveRetrievalResult:
2335:     """Execute live retrieval and classify the corpus.
2336: 
2337:     Args:
2338:         research_question: Raw user query (anchor for scope validator).
2339:         amplified_queries: Optional extra queries (scope-validated).
2340:         protocol: Protocol dict; used for scope validation.
2341:         max_serper: Max Serper results per query.
2342:         max_s2: Max S2 results per query.
2343:         fetch_cap: Hard cap on total URLs to fetch (cost control).
2344:         enable_openalex_enrich: Toggle OpenAlex lookup per URL.
2345:         enable_prefetch_filter: Toggle embedding-based off-topic filter
2346:             (slow; off by default for the first live run).
2347:         domain: Optional scope-template domain name (clinical / policy /
2348:             tech / due_diligence). When set, R-6 Gap-2 domain backends
2349:             augment the generic Serper+S2 retrieval with arxiv (tech),
2350:             SEC EDGAR (DD), or policy-site targeted Serper queries.
2351:         research_frame: Optional planner `ResearchFrame` (I-meta-005 Phase 2
2352:             #986). When set (ON-mode), the field-agnostic need-type registry
2353:             REPLACES the domain backends at the Step-2a seam — discovery is
2354:             keyed on the frame's declared `evidence_needs` + extracted
2355:             `jurisdictions`, NOT a domain. Mutually exclusive with `domain` on
2356:             the on-path (the sweep passes `domain=None` on-mode).
2357:         anchor_seed: When True (default — OFF-mode byte-identical), the verbatim
2358:             `research_question` is PREPENDED to the effective query list and the
2359:             scope validator keeps it (`always_keep_anchor=True`). I-meta-005
2360:             Phase 4 (#988) GAP rounds pass `anchor_seed=False` so the broad anchor
2361:             is NOT re-fired: `all_queries = amplified_queries` ONLY, the scope
2362:             validator does NOT re-add the anchor (`always_keep_anchor=False`), and
2363:             the need-type backend is invoked with `anchor_seed=False` (no
2364:             `research_question` prepend there either). A gap round therefore fires
2365:             EXACTLY the gap sub-queries on BOTH the core Serper/S2 seam AND the
2366:             need-type adapters.
2367: 
2368:     Returns LiveRetrievalResult.
2369: 
2370:     Raises:
2371:         MalformedPlanError: when `research_frame` carries a malformed
2372:             `evidence_needs` value OR a malformed jurisdiction SHAPE. This is
2373:             validated UP-FRONT (before ANY live discovery, incl. core
2374:             Serper/S2) and FAILS LOUD — it NEVER silently degrades to core
2375:             Serper/S2 (brief §2.4 P2-note-1). Distinct from the fail-OPEN
2376:             adapter/network handling at the Step-2a seam.
2377:     """
2378:     api_calls: dict[str, int] = {"serper": 0, "s2": 0, "openalex": 0, "fetch": 0}
2379:     notes: list[str] = []
2380: 
2381:     # ── Step 0: UP-FRONT plan validation (I-meta-005 Phase 2, P2-note-1) ──
2382:     # A malformed frame (bad evidence_need / bad jurisdiction SHAPE) must FAIL
2383:     # LOUD here — BEFORE the core Serper/S2 baseline spends or populates any
2384:     # candidate. The router's validation raises MalformedPlanError; we let it
2385:     # propagate (it is a VALIDATION error, distinct from the fail-OPEN
2386:     # adapter/network handling at Step 2a). Only fires on-mode (frame present).
2387:     if research_frame is not None and not seed_only:
2388:         from src.polaris_graph.discovery.need_type_router import (
2389:             validate_frame_needs,
2390:         )
2391:         # Raises MalformedPlanError on a malformed value/SHAPE; a valid-shape
2392:         # unknown jurisdiction + an empty evidence_needs both pass (non-fatal).
2393:         validate_frame_needs(research_frame)
2394: 
2395:     # ── Step 1: compile the effective query list ──────────────────────
2396:     # I-meta-005 Phase 4 (#988): `anchor_seed=False` (gap rounds) suppresses the
2397:     # broad `research_question` anchor on BOTH the prepend AND the scope-validator
2398:     # reinsertion, so a gap round fires ONLY the gap sub-queries (no wasted
2399:     # anchor re-run). Default True = OFF-mode byte-identical.
2400:     all_queries: list[str] = [research_question] if anchor_seed else []
2401:     if amplified_queries:
2402:         all_queries.extend(amplified_queries)
2403:     # Scope validation (de-drift)
2404:     if protocol:
2405:         valid = validate_amplified_queries(
2406:             all_queries, protocol, always_keep_anchor=anchor_seed,
2407:         )
2408:         effective_queries = valid.kept
2409:         notes.append(
2410:             f"scope_query_validator: {len(valid.kept)} kept / "
2411:             f"{len(valid.dropped)} dropped"
2412:         )
2413:     else:
2414:         effective_queries = list(all_queries)
2415: 
2416:     # ── Step 2: run Serper + S2 across queries ──────────────────────
2417:     seen_urls: set[str] = set()
2418:     candidates: list[SearchCandidate] = []
2419: 
2420:     # I-bug-776 (#817) layer-4 (Codex decision b): direct primary-trial DOI seed
2421:     # candidates. Injected at the FRONT so the fetch_cap slice always includes
2422:     # them (a reserved anchored-primary lane), and fetch_cap is bumped by the
2423:     # seed count below so they are ADDITIVE — they do not evict search/guideline
2424:     # candidates. They pass the SAME fetch / Unpaywall-OA / extraction / tier /
2425:     # adequacy gates as every other source: a seed counts as T1 ONLY if the tier
2426:     # classifier identifies the fetched content as a primary trial (no laundering).
2427:     _n_seed_injected = 0
2428:     for _surl in seed_urls or []:
2429:         if _surl and _surl not in seen_urls:
2430:             seen_urls.add(_surl)
2431:             # FX-15a (#1118): the seed SOURCE/ORIGIN labels are now caller-supplied so the agentic
2432:             # lane (seed_source='agentic_seed') is no longer mislabeled as a primary-trial DOI seed.
2433:             # Defaults preserve the #817 layer-4 DOI-lane labels for every existing caller.
2434:             candidates.append(SearchCandidate(
2435:                 url=_surl, title="", snippet="", source=seed_source,
2436:                 query_origin=seed_query_origin,
2437:             ))
2438:             _n_seed_injected += 1
2439:     if _n_seed_injected:
2440:         logger.info(
2441:             "[live_retriever] injected %d direct seed candidates (source=%s, query_origin=%s)",
2442:             _n_seed_injected, seed_source, seed_query_origin,
2443:         )
2444: 
2445:     # I-meta-002-q1d (#942-deepener, Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1): seed_only processes ONLY the injected
2446:     # seed_urls — no Serper/S2 fan-out and no domain backends. Used by the deepener pass so it fetches
2447:     # exactly the citation-snowball-discovered URLs (and nothing else) through the same chokepoint.
2448:     for q in ([] if seed_only else effective_queries):
2449:         logger.info("[live_retriever] SERPER q=%r", q[:80])
2450:         # FX-17 (#1126) iter-2: pass api_calls so each HTTP page (not each query) is counted inside
2451:         # _serper_search. The old `+= 1` here undercounted paginated breadth.
2452:         serper_hits = _serper_search(q, num=max_serper, api_calls=api_calls)
2453:         for hit in serper_hits:
2454:             url = hit.get("url", "")
2455:             if not url or url in seen_urls:
2456:                 continue
2457:             seen_urls.add(url)
2458:             candidates.append(SearchCandidate(
2459:                 url=url,
2460:                 title=hit.get("title", ""),
2461:                 snippet=hit.get("snippet", ""),
2462:                 source="serper",
2463:                 query_origin=q,
2464:             ))
2465: 
2466:         # FX-18 (#1122): S2 bulk is a KEYWORD index — feeding it the 40-70-word NL query returned ~0
2467:         # for 4/5 golden questions. Send a SHORT content-keyword distillation of `q` instead (pure,
2468:         # stopword-filtered, capped). Flag-gated PG_S2_KEYWORD_DISTILL (default on); an empty
2469:         # distillation falls back to the NL `q` (never an empty search). The candidate's `query_origin`
2470:         # stays the NL `q` so the per-sub-query rerank reservation + plan-sufficiency are unchanged.
2471:         _s2_query = q
2472:         if os.getenv("PG_S2_KEYWORD_DISTILL", "1") != "0":
2473:             _kw = distill_keywords(q, max_terms=int(os.getenv("PG_S2_KEYWORD_MAX_TERMS", "8")))
2474:             if _kw:
2475:                 _s2_query = _kw
2476:         logger.info("[live_retriever] S2 q=%r", _s2_query[:80])
2477:         s2_hits = _s2_bulk_search(_s2_query, limit=max_s2)
2478:         api_calls["s2"] += 1
2479:         for hit in s2_hits:
2480:             url = hit.get("url", "")
2481:             if not url or url in seen_urls:
2482:                 continue
2483:             seen_urls.add(url)
2484:             candidates.append(SearchCandidate(
2485:                 url=url,
2486:                 title=hit.get("title", ""),
2487:                 snippet=hit.get("snippet", ""),
2488:                 source="s2",
2489:                 metadata={"doi": hit.get("doi"), "year": hit.get("year")},
2490:                 query_origin=q,
2491:             ))
2492: 
2493:         # FX-18 (#1122): OpenAlex /works?search handles NL queries that the S2 keyword index does not,
2494:         # and is already built (domain_backends.openalex_search, fail-open). Wire it as a PARALLEL
2495:         # academic backend — ADD (union), not replace S2 (Codex Q8) — sending the NL `q`; union+dedup
2496:         # via the shared `seen_urls`. Candidates carry source="openalex_search"; default query_origin
2497:         # to `q`. Flag-gated PG_OPENALEX_SEARCH (default on). Fail-open: a backend fault adds 0 hits.
2498:         if os.getenv("PG_OPENALEX_SEARCH", "1") != "0":
2499:             _oa_t0 = time.time()
2500:             try:
2501:                 from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.domain_backends import (  # noqa: E402
2502:                     openalex_search,
2503:                 )
2504:                 _oa_hits = openalex_search(q, limit=max_s2)
2505:                 api_calls["openalex_search"] = api_calls.get("openalex_search", 0) + 1
2506:                 # FX-20 (#1128): trace openalex as a first-class backend so the discovery_funnel reads
2507:                 # its requested-vs-returned from the SAME tool_trace rows as serper/s2 (FX-18 wired the
2508:                 # call but never traced it — a tracer-only funnel would silently OMIT openalex).
2509:                 _trace_tool(
2510:                     "openalex_search", target=q, status="ok",
2511:                     latency_ms=(time.time() - _oa_t0) * 1000.0,
2512:                     backend_used="openalex_works_api",
2513:                     result_count=len(_oa_hits), num_requested=max_s2,
2514:                 )
2515:                 # FX-18b (#1123): mirror serper/s2 -> emit an openalex retrieval_trace row so RERUN §-1.1 can verify it fired.
2516:                 _trace_query("openalex_search", q, [getattr(c, "url", "") for c in _oa_hits])
2517:                 for cand in _oa_hits:
2518:                     url = getattr(cand, "url", "")
2519:                     if not url or url in seen_urls:
2520:                         continue
2521:                     seen_urls.add(url)
2522:                     if not getattr(cand, "query_origin", ""):
2523:                         cand.query_origin = q
2524:                     candidates.append(cand)
2525:             except Exception as exc:
2526:                 _trace_tool(
2527:                     "openalex_search", target=q, status="fail",
2528:                     latency_ms=(time.time() - _oa_t0) * 1000.0,
2529:                     backend_used="openalex_works_api",
2530:                     error=str(exc), result_count=0, num_requested=max_s2,
2531:                 )
2532:                 logger.warning(
2533:                     "[live_retriever] openalex_search failed for %r (fail-open): %s",
2534:                     q[:60], exc,
2535:                 )
2536: 
2537:     # ── Step 2a: specialized issuer-class backends ──────────────────
2538:     # I-meta-005 Phase 2 (#986) DUAL PATH:
2539:     #   ON-mode (research_frame present): the field-agnostic NEED-TYPE registry
2540:     #     REPLACES the domain backends — discovery is routed off the frame's
2541:     #     declared evidence_needs + jurisdictions (NO `if domain ==` branch
2542:     #     reached). The malformed-frame case already FAILED LOUD at Step 0.
2543:     #   OFF-mode (domain set, no frame): the legacy R-6 Gap-2 domain switch runs
2544:     #     byte-identically (arXiv for tech, SEC EDGAR for DD, policy-site Serper
2545:     #     for policy, Europe PMC for clinical).
2546:     # Both stay fail-OPEN at the live seam (ADAPTER/network exception -> 0 new
2547:     # hits; the run degrades to the core Serper/S2 baseline). Skipped on the
2548:     # seed_only deepener pass (no extra retrieval).
2549:     if research_frame is not None and not seed_only:
2550:         # ON-path: need-type registry dispatch. NO domain literal consulted.
2551:         try:
2552:             from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.domain_backends import (  # noqa: E402
2553:                 run_need_type_backends,
2554:             )
2555:             need_result = run_need_type_backends(
2556:                 frame=research_frame,
2557:                 research_question=research_question,
2558:                 amplified_queries=amplified_queries,
2559:                 anchor_seed=anchor_seed,
2560:             )
2561:             for cand in need_result.candidates:
2562:                 url = cand.url
2563:                 if not url or url in seen_urls:
2564:                     continue
2565:                 seen_urls.add(url)
2566:                 if not getattr(cand, "query_origin", ""):
2567:                     cand.query_origin = "need_type_backend"
2568:                 candidates.append(cand)
2569:             if need_result.backends_used:
2570:                 notes.append(
2571:                     f"need_type_backends({need_result.needs}): "
2572:                     f"{need_result.per_backend_counts}"
2573:                 )
2574:                 for backend_name in need_result.backends_used:
2575:                     api_calls[backend_name] = (
2576:                         api_calls.get(backend_name, 0) + 1
2577:                     )
2578:         except Exception as exc:
2579:             # ADAPTER/network fail-open ONLY. A MalformedPlanError cannot reach
2580:             # here — it raised at Step 0 before the baseline ran.
2581:             logger.warning(
2582:                 "[live_retriever] need_type_backends failed (fail-open): %s",
2583:                 exc,
2584:             )
2585:     elif domain and not seed_only:
2586:         try:
2587:             from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.domain_backends import (  # noqa: E402
2588:                 run_domain_backends,
2589:             )
2590:             domain_result = run_domain_backends(
2591:                 domain=domain,
2592:                 research_question=research_question,
2593:                 amplified_queries=amplified_queries,
2594:             )
2595:             for cand in domain_result.candidates:
2596:                 url = cand.url
2597:                 if not url or url in seen_urls:
2598:                     continue
2599:                 seen_urls.add(url)
2600:                 # I-meta-002-q1d (#951): give domain-backend candidates a stable origin
2601:                 # bucket so the per-sub-query rerank reservation handles them consistently.
2602:                 if not getattr(cand, "query_origin", ""):
2603:                     cand.query_origin = "domain_backend"
2604:                 candidates.append(cand)
2605:             if domain_result.backends_used:
2606:                 notes.append(
2607:                     f"domain_backends({domain}): "
2608:                     f"{domain_result.per_backend_counts}"
2609:                 )
2610:                 for backend_name in domain_result.backends_used:
2611:                     api_calls[backend_name] = (
2612:                         api_calls.get(backend_name, 0) + 1
2613:                     )
2614:         except Exception as exc:
2615:             logger.warning(
2616:                 "[live_retriever] domain_backends failed for %r: %s",
2617:                 domain, exc,
2618:             )
2619: 
2620:     total_pre_filter = len(candidates)
2621:     logger.info("[live_retriever] %d unique candidates from search", total_pre_filter)
2622: 
2623:     # ── Step 3: prefetch off-topic filter ──────────────────────────
2624:     if enable_prefetch_filter and candidates:
2625:         # FX-15b (#1119): EXCLUDE the injected seeds (empty title/snippet, source in
2626:         # _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS) from the semantic off-topic filter. They have no snippet to embed,
2627:         # so `filter_search_results` would score them ~0 similarity and REJECT the entire reserved
2628:         # seed lane (e.g. all agentic seeds). Filter only the non-seed search candidates; re-prepend
2629:         # the seeds untouched (mirrors the seed split in `_rerank_and_reserve`). Seeds are never
2630:         # off-topic-dropped — exactly as before this lane could enable the filter.
2631:         _seed_cands = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
2632:         _nonseed_cands = [
2633:             c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") not in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS
2634:         ]
2635:         if _nonseed_cands:
2636:             _pre_offtopic_urls = {c.url for c in _nonseed_cands}
2637:             filt = filter_search_results(_nonseed_cands, research_question)
2638:             candidates = _seed_cands + filt.kept
2639:             for _dropped_url in _pre_offtopic_urls - {c.url for c in filt.kept}:
2640:                 _trace_drop(_dropped_url, "offtopic")
2641:             notes.append(
2642:                 f"prefetch_offtopic: {filt.total_kept} kept / "
2643:                 f"{filt.total_rejected} rejected (threshold={filt.threshold_used:.2f})"
2644:             )
2645:         # else: only seeds present (e.g. seed_only mode) — nothing to off-topic filter.
2646:     kept_by_offtopic = len(candidates)
2647: 
2648:     # ── Step 4: fetch-time relevance rerank + per-sub-query reservation, then cap ──
2649:     # I-meta-002-q1d (#951, #943): replace arrival-order truncation with a no-spend,
2650:     # no-model-load lexical relevance rerank that reserves >=1 slot per sub-query so a
2651:     # long full-paragraph query cannot monopolize the cap (the breadth of amplified
2652:     # queries was previously illusory). I-bug-776 (#817) layer-4 seed lane preserved:
2653:     # primary-trial DOI seeds (empty title/snippet) are split out and prepended AFTER
2654:     # ranking so relevance scoring can never drop them — they remain additive.
2655:     _pre_rerank_urls = {c.url for c in candidates}
2656:     candidates = _rerank_and_reserve(
2657:         candidates,
2658:         research_question=research_question,
2659:         fetch_cap=fetch_cap,
2660:         n_seed_injected=_n_seed_injected,
2661:     )
2662:     for _dropped_url in _pre_rerank_urls - {c.url for c in candidates}:
2663:         _trace_drop(_dropped_url, "rerank_not_selected")
2664: 
2665:     classified_sources: list[CorpusSource] = []
2666:     evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
2667:     fetched = 0
2668:     failed_fetch = 0
2669:     # I-ready-017 #1134: journal_only metadata sidecar (ON-path only). Keyed by
2670:     # canonical URL; carries per-source journal-article signals for the
2671:     # citeability predicate. Stays None when the flag is OFF (byte-identical).
2672:     from src.polaris_graph.nodes.journal_only_filter import (
2673:         journal_only_flag_enabled as _jo_flag_enabled,
2674:         journal_metadata_entry as _jo_meta_entry,
2675:         canonicalize_url as _jo_canon,
2676:     )
2677:     _journal_only_on = _jo_flag_enabled()
2678:     _journal_sidecar: dict[str, Any] = {} if _journal_only_on else {}
2679: 
2680:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
2681:     # M-INT-1 — Parallel fetch into live_retriever (Phase E1)
2682:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
2683:     # Wires `parallel_fetch.parallel_fetch(...)` into the content-fetch
2684:     # loop. Per FINAL_PLAN.md M-INT-1: imported, invoked, run-log
2685:     # evidence, and PG_USE_PARALLEL_FETCH=0 disables (rollback).
2686:     #
2687:     # The substrate's ParallelFetcher Protocol expects (bytes, str,
2688:     # int). Live retrieval's existing _fetch_content returns
2689:     # (content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld) — we wrap it in an adapter
2690:     # that stashes the full 5-tuple in a side dict keyed by URL,
2691:     # then post-process serially using the side dict. Diff-gate P1-C: the 5th
2692:     # element is the raw ld+json captured before _strip_html (Signal C input).
2693:     use_parallel = os.environ.get("PG_USE_PARALLEL_FETCH", "1") != "0"
2694:     fetched_side: dict[str, tuple[str, bool, str, str, str]] = {}
2695: 
2696:     if use_parallel and candidates:
2697:         from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.parallel_fetch import (
2698:             FetchTask,
2699:             parallel_fetch,
2700:         )
2701: 
2702:         class _LiveContentParallelFetcher:
2703:             """Adapter wrapping `_fetch_content(url, max_chars)` for
2704:             the parallel_fetch substrate's ParallelFetcher Protocol.
2705:             Stashes the full 5-tuple (content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld)
2706:             in a thread-safe side dict so the post-processing loop
2707:             can read it back per-candidate."""
2708: 
2709:             def __init__(self, max_chars: int) -> None:
2710:                 self.max_chars = max_chars
2711:                 self._lock = threading.Lock()
2712:                 self.results = fetched_side
2713: 
2714:             def fetch(
2715:                 self, task: "FetchTask"
2716:             ) -> tuple[bytes, str, int]:
2717:                 # I-meta-007c: surface the per-candidate DOI/PMID hints
2718:                 # (stashed in task_metadata at task construction) so the OA
2719:                 # resolver can fire on the DEFAULT parallel path, not just the
2720:                 # serial fallback.
2721:                 _meta = task.task_metadata or {}
2722:                 content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld = _fetch_content(
2723:                     task.source_url, self.max_chars,
2724:                     doi_hint=str(_meta.get("doi") or ""),
2725:                     pmid_hint=str(_meta.get("pmid") or ""),
2726:                 )
2727:                 with self._lock:
2728:                     self.results[task.source_url] = (
2729:                         content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld,
2730:                     )
2731:                 payload = (content or "").encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
2732:                 return (payload, "text/plain", 200 if ok else 502)
2733: 
2734:         try:
2735:             max_workers = int(os.environ.get(
2736:                 "PG_LIVE_RETRIEVER_MAX_WORKERS", "8",
2737:             ))
2738:         except ValueError:
2739:             max_workers = 8
2740:         try:
2741:             per_task_timeout = float(os.environ.get(
2742:                 "PG_LIVE_RETRIEVER_FETCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "120",
2743:             ))
2744:         except ValueError:
2745:             per_task_timeout = 120.0
2746: 
2747:         fetch_tasks = []
2748:         for idx, c in enumerate(candidates):
2749:             # I-meta-007c: carry the candidate's DOI/PMID hints into the
2750:             # FetchTask so _LiveContentParallelFetcher.fetch can pass them to
2751:             # _fetch_content for the OA resolver (default = parallel path).
2752:             _doi, _pmid = _candidate_oa_hints(getattr(c, "metadata", None))
2753:             fetch_tasks.append(
2754:                 FetchTask(
2755:                     source_url=c.url,
2756:                     backend_id="default",
2757:                     task_metadata={"index": idx, "doi": _doi, "pmid": _pmid},
2758:                 )
2759:             )
2760:         fetcher = _LiveContentParallelFetcher(DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS)
2761:         parallel_report = parallel_fetch(
2762:             fetch_tasks, fetcher,
2763:             max_workers=max_workers,
2764:             per_task_timeout=per_task_timeout,
2765:         )
2766:         # Run-log evidence: persist the substrate's report into
2767:         # api_calls so the manifest sees a non-zero invocation count.
2768:         api_calls["parallel_fetch_success_count"] = (
2769:             parallel_report.success_count
2770:         )
2771:         api_calls["parallel_fetch_errored_count"] = (
2772:             parallel_report.errored_count
2773:         )
2774:         api_calls["parallel_fetch_timeout_count"] = (
2775:             parallel_report.timeout_count
2776:         )
2777:         logger.info(
2778:             "[live_retriever] M-INT-1 parallel_fetch: %d success, "
2779:             "%d errored, %d timeout (max_workers=%d, "
2780:             "per_task_timeout=%.0fs)",
2781:             parallel_report.success_count,
2782:             parallel_report.errored_count,
2783:             parallel_report.timeout_count,
2784:             max_workers, per_task_timeout,
2785:         )
2786: 
2787:     # #554 (I-bug-115): bound the synchronous post-fetch candidate loop so a
2788:     # wedged per-candidate operation can never hang the run with no terminal
2789:     # verdict. Layer 1 = per-candidate enrich bound (_bounded_openalex_enrich);
2790:     # Layer 2 = this overall wall-clock budget; Layer 3 = per-candidate
2791:     # progress logging below so any future loop stall is diagnosable.
2792:     # I-ready-003 (#1074) P1: the post-fetch loop processes ALL ~fetch_cap candidates serially (each
2793:     # paying an OpenAlex enrich round-trip + tier-classify). A FIXED budget that does NOT scale with
2794:     # fetch_cap silently TRUNCATES the corpus at full cap (the I-cap-005 ~40-URL silent-throttle class,
2795:     # one layer deeper): the slate raised fetch_cap 25x without scaling this wall-clock budget, so the
2796:     # loop hit the deadline mid-corpus, set corpus_truncated, and broke — a "1000-URL" run classified
2797:     # only the first few hundred. Scale the budget with fetch_cap: max(explicit env floor, fetch_cap *
2798:     # per-URL budget). BYTE-IDENTICAL for small caps (the 900s env floor wins when fetch_cap is small);
2799:     # a conservative operator env can no longer silently win because the fetch_cap term floors it.
2800:     _loop_deadline = time.monotonic() + _post_fetch_loop_budget(fetch_cap)
2801:     _enrich_failfast = _env_int("PG_OPENALEX_ENRICH_FAILFAST", 3)
2802:     _enrich_stats: dict[str, int] = {}
2803:     _enrich_disabled = False
2804: 
2805:     # #958 (S2): corpus-truncation counters. Initialized BEFORE the loop so the
2806:     # empty/no-break path does not depend on a loop-local `i` (Codex P2). Default
2807:     # = "processed all" / not truncated; the budget-break below overrides.
2808:     _corpus_truncated = False
2809:     _candidates_total = len(candidates)
2810:     _candidates_processed = len(candidates)
2811: 
2812:     for i, cand in enumerate(candidates):
2813:         if time.monotonic() > _loop_deadline:
2814:             logger.warning(
2815:                 "[live_retriever] post-fetch loop budget exceeded — stopping "
2816:                 "at candidate %d/%d (%d already classified)",
2817:                 i, len(candidates), len(classified_sources),
2818:             )
2819:             # #958: record the truncation as a fail-loud signal (was log-only).
2820:             # candidates_processed = the zero-based break index i = post-filter
2821:             # candidates whose loop iteration began before the cutoff (Codex P2).
2822:             _corpus_truncated = True
2823:             _candidates_processed = i
2824:             break
2825:         logger.info(
2826:             "[live_retriever] post-fetch candidate %d/%d %s",
2827:             i + 1, len(candidates), cand.url[:60],
2828:         )
2829:         if use_parallel:
2830:             content, ok, content_title_from_fetch, body_article_type, raw_jsonld = (
2831:                 fetched_side.get(cand.url, ("", False, "", "", ""))
2832:             )
2833:         else:
2834:             # Fallback serial path (PG_USE_PARALLEL_FETCH=0).
2835:             # Rate-limit gently (Serper doesn't but S2 prefers <= 1rps)
2836:             if i > 0 and i % 5 == 0:
2837:                 time.sleep(0.2)
2838:             # I-meta-007c: thread the DOI/PMID hints from S2 metadata so the
2839:             # OA resolver can fire when AccessBypass returns a stub/paywall.
2840:             _cand_doi, _cand_pmid = _candidate_oa_hints(
2841:                 getattr(cand, "metadata", None)
2842:             )
2843:             # Fetch content (for tier classification + evidence)
2844:             content, ok, content_title_from_fetch, body_article_type, raw_jsonld = (
2845:                 _fetch_content(
2846:                     cand.url, DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS,
2847:                     doi_hint=_cand_doi, pmid_hint=_cand_pmid,
2848:                 )
2849:             )
2850:         api_calls["fetch"] += 1
2851:         if not ok:
2852:             failed_fetch += 1
2853:             _trace_drop(cand.url, "fetch_failed")
2854:         else:
2855:             fetched += 1
2856: 
2857:         # Optional OpenAlex enrichment — wall-clock-bounded (#554). After
2858:         # PG_OPENALEX_ENRICH_FAILFAST timeouts in this run, stop attempting
2859:         # enrichment: it prevents abandoned daemon threads from accumulating
2860:         # when OpenAlex is degraded for the whole run.
2861:         oa = {}
2862:         if enable_openalex_enrich and not _enrich_disabled:
2863:             oa = _bounded_openalex_enrich(cand.url, cand.title, _enrich_stats)
2864:             if oa:
2865:                 api_calls["openalex"] += 1
2866:             if _enrich_stats.get("enrich_timeouts", 0) >= _enrich_failfast:
2867:                 _enrich_disabled = True
2868:                 logger.warning(
2869:                     "[live_retriever] OpenAlex enrich timed out %dx — "
2870:                     "disabling enrichment for the rest of this run",
2871:                     _enrich_stats["enrich_timeouts"],
2872:                 )
2873: 
2874:         # Classify via tier_classifier
2875:         domain_ = _domain_of(cand.url)
2876:         # BUG-M-12 / M-13 (Codex pass 12/13): title resolution order
2877:         # (longest → most reliable):
2878:         #   1. OpenAlex display_name (full title from DOI lookup when
2879:         #      URL embeds a DOI; otherwise from title-search fallback)
2880:         #   2. Content-extracted title from fetched page (Jina/Crawl4AI
2881:         #      markdown or HTML <title>) — catches MDPI/JAMA/PMC URLs
2882:         #      where DOI isn't in the URL path
2883:         #   3. Serper snippet title (often truncated)
2884:         # Existing detectors (_detect_systematic_review_from_title,
2885:         # _detect_narrative_flavor_from_title) then see the full
2886:         # suffix and demote correctly.
2887:         # BUG-M-14 (Codex pass 14): use the title extracted at fetch
2888:         # time (from raw content BEFORE _strip_html stripped tags)
2889:         # rather than trying to re-extract from the already-stripped
2890:         # text. Fall back to the content-based extraction on stripped
2891:         # text in case fetch didn't populate it.
2892:         content_title = content_title_from_fetch or _extract_title_from_content(content)
2893:         openalex_title = oa.get("openalex_full_title", "") or ""
2894:         # Pick the longest candidate — longer titles carry more signal
2895:         # (SR/MA / perspective / guidance suffixes).
2896:         title_candidates = [t for t in (openalex_title, content_title, cand.title) if t]
2897:         if title_candidates:
2898:             classifier_title = max(title_candidates, key=len)
2899:         else:
2900:             classifier_title = cand.title or ""
2901:         # Phase 0a (C1/C5): reconstruct the additive AuthoritySignals payload
2902:         # from the live-path enrich dict. Absent/partial -> None / partial ->
2903:         # the authority model returns LOW confidence (fail-honest). Inert on the
2904:         # OFF path (the legacy rule body never reads `.authority`).
2905:         authority_payload = None
2906:         _auth_dict = oa.get("authority_signals")
2907:         if isinstance(_auth_dict, dict):
2908:             authority_payload = AuthoritySignals(
2909:                 cited_by_count=_auth_dict.get("cited_by_count"),
2910:                 source_id=_auth_dict.get("source_id", "") or "",
2911:                 venue_summary_stats=_auth_dict.get("venue_summary_stats"),
2912:                 is_core=_auth_dict.get("is_core"),
2913:                 is_in_doaj=_auth_dict.get("is_in_doaj"),
2914:                 apc_prices=_auth_dict.get("apc_prices"),
2915:                 publication_year=_auth_dict.get("publication_year"),
2916:                 ror_id=_auth_dict.get("ror_id", "") or "",
2917:                 institution_type=_auth_dict.get("institution_type", "") or "",
2918:                 country_code=_auth_dict.get("country_code", "") or "",
2919:             )
2920:         # I-ready-017 #1134: resolve the article DOI for the journal_only
2921:         # citeability predicate (ADDITIVE — sourced from the candidate's OA
2922:         # hints; "" when unknown). Cheap, no network.
2923:         _jo_doi, _jo_pmid = _candidate_oa_hints(getattr(cand, "metadata", None))
2924:         signals = ClassificationSignals(
2925:             url=cand.url,
2926:             title=classifier_title,
2927:             publisher="",
2928:             fetched_content_length=len(content),
2929:             openalex_publication_type=oa.get("openalex_pub_type", "") or "",
2930:             openalex_source_type=oa.get("openalex_source_type", "") or "",
2931:             openalex_is_peer_reviewed=bool(oa.get("is_peer_reviewed", False)),
2932:             doi=str(_jo_doi or ""),
2933:             source_type_hint="",
2934:             # BUG-M-17 (Codex pass 2): body-inspection secondary signal.
2935:             body_article_type=body_article_type,
2936:             authority=authority_payload,
2937:             # Diff-gate P1-B/P1-C: wire the structural junk inputs so Signal C
2938:             # (junk_detection) can actually fire on the ON path. INERT when OFF
2939:             # (the legacy rule body never reads these).
2940:             #   * fetched_body = the stripped visible page text (survives
2941:             #     _strip_html; carries press-release / login-wall body cues).
2942:             #   * structured_jsonld = the RAW ld+json <script> block contents
2943:             #     captured by _fetch_content BEFORE _strip_html deleted the
2944:             #     <script> blocks (P1-C). Without this the NewsArticle @type
2945:             #     marker never reaches the classifier on the live path — it is
2946:             #     destroyed by stripping. Honestly empty when the fetch backend
2947:             #     already returned cleaned text (Jina/Crawl4AI) with no JSON-LD.
2948:             #   * claim_vendor_token = the lowercased research question, used by
2949:             #     the self-interest check (exact host-org-token == vendor-token
2950:             #     equality, so a phrase can never false-fire). Extracting single
2951:             #     candidate vendor tokens from the question is a Gate-A residual.
2952:             fetched_body=content or "",
2953:             structured_jsonld=raw_jsonld or "",
2954:             claim_vendor_token=(research_question or "").strip().lower(),
2955:         )
2956:         tier_result = classify_source_tier(signals)
2957: 
2958:         classified_sources.append(CorpusSource(
2959:             url=cand.url,
2960:             title=cand.title,
2961:             domain=domain_,
2962:             tier=tier_result.tier.value,
2963:             tier_confidence=tier_result.confidence,
2964:             tier_rule=tier_result.matched_rules[0] if tier_result.matched_rules else "",
2965:             tier_reasons=list(tier_result.reasons),
2966:         ))
2967: 
2968:         # I-ready-017 #1134: record the per-source journal-article signals into
2969:         # the journal_only sidecar (ON-path only; keyed by canonical URL). The

2213:         # not reliably exclude clinical cohort queries ("population of asthmatics/smokers/diabetics"), so

2217:         # fetch-cap reduction cannot breach that bar. The adequacy-relaxation half (a right-sized ANSWER

2287:             def _planner_llm(prompt: str) -> str:
2288:                 # Production Writer call. Build + smoke NEVER reach this path
2289:                 # (the planner callable is injected/faked there). One Writer
2290:                 # call (plus at most one bounded retry inside plan_research).
2291:                 #
2292:                 # `run_one_query` is async — the sweep event loop is already
2293:                 # running here — so the coroutine is driven on a SEPARATE
2294:                 # thread with its own loop (thread-safe; never touches the
2295:                 # running loop, which `run_until_complete` would crash on).
2296:                 #
2297:                 # I-meta-005 Phase 1 FIX 3 (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 #3): the
2298:                 # prior bare `ThreadPoolExecutor(...).submit(asyncio.run, ...)`
2299:                 # ran with the worker's OWN empty ContextVar state, so the
2300:                 # planner Writer call's billed cost accumulated in
2301:                 # `_RUN_COST_CTX` only inside the worker snapshot and was LOST
2302:                 # to the parent run (`current_run_cost()` / `manifest.cost_usd`
2303:                 # under-reported live planner spend — a budget-cap integrity
2304:                 # LAW violation). Fix mirrors `auto_induction.llm_inductor`
2305:                 # rounds 3-4 (and `scope_classifier_llm._run_async_in_isolated_
2306:                 # thread`): capture the parent context with
2307:                 # `contextvars.copy_context()` and run the worker inside that
2308:                 # snapshot via `parent_ctx.run()` (READ visibility), THEN apply
2309:                 # the worker's cost delta back to the parent context via a
2310:                 # closure-shared holder (write-back, fires whether or not the
2311:                 # call raised — the OpenRouter client bills partial cost before
2312:                 # raising on empty-content/retry).
2313:                 import asyncio as _asyncio
2314:                 import concurrent.futures as _futures
2315:                 import contextvars as _contextvars
2316:                 from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
2317:                     _RUN_COST_CTX,
2318:                 )
2319: 
2320:                 _parent_cost_before = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
2321:                 _worker_cost_after_holder: list[float] = [_parent_cost_before]
2322: 
2323:                 async def _run() -> str:
2324:                     _client = OpenRouterClient(model=PG_GENERATOR_MODEL)
2325:                     try:
2326:                         _resp = await _client.generate(
2327:                             prompt=prompt, max_tokens=2000, temperature=0.2,
2328:                         )
2329:                         return (_resp.content or "").strip()
2330:                     finally:
2331:                         # Capture the worker snapshot's accumulated cost even
2332:                         # on raise (OpenRouter bills partial cost before
2333:                         # raising on empty-content/retry paths).
2334:                         _worker_cost_after_holder[0] = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
2335:                         if hasattr(_client, "close"):
2336:                             try:
2337:                                 await _client.close()
2338:                             except Exception:
2339:                                 pass
2340: 
2341:                 _parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()
2342: 
2343:                 def _worker() -> str:
2344:                     def _run_under_ctx() -> str:
2345:                         return _asyncio.run(_run())
2346:                     return _parent_ctx.run(_run_under_ctx)
2347: 
2348:                 try:
2349:                     with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as _pool:
2350:                         return _pool.submit(_worker).result()
2351:                 finally:
2352:                     # Apply the worker's cost delta to the parent context so
2353:                     # the planner Writer spend merges into the parent run cost
2354:                     # (whether or not the worker raised).
2355:                     _cost_delta = (
2356:                         _worker_cost_after_holder[0] - _parent_cost_before
2357:                     )
2358:                     if _cost_delta > 0:
2359:                         _RUN_COST_CTX.set(_parent_cost_before + _cost_delta)
2360: 
2361:             _research_plan = plan_research(
2362:                 q["question"], planner_llm=_planner_llm,
2363:             )

2365:             _plan_canonical = serialize_plan_canonical(_research_plan)

2511:             # tasks each get their OWN copied context. ContextVars do NOT propagate cost UP from
2512:             # concurrent children, so STORM's _RUN_COST_CTX spend can be captured NEITHER by a parent
2513:             # copy_context read NOR by a post-hoc check_run_budget — it would silently bypass
2514:             # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN. Robust fix: book a CONSERVATIVE cost ENVELOPE (scaled by the STORM
2515:             # perspective/round config) into the parent run cost and ENFORCE the cap BEFORE STORM runs.
2516:             # STORM then runs in an ISOLATED context (its real spend is discarded from the parent total —
2517:             # the envelope IS the parent's accounting for STORM). The envelope-inclusive isolated context
2518:             # also makes STORM's OWN internal budget checks stop early if actual spend nears the cap.
2519:             # Conservative by design: over-books, never under-enforces (LAW VI). The per-round estimate
2520:             # is env-tunable.
2521:             _storm_perspectives = int(os.getenv("PG_STORM_PERSPECTIVES_COUNT", "8"))
2522:             _storm_rounds = int(os.getenv("PG_STORM_ROUNDS_PER_PERSPECTIVE", "4"))
2523:             _storm_per_round_usd = float(os.getenv("PG_STORM_PER_ROUND_COST_USD", "0.10"))
2524:             _storm_cost_envelope = max(
2525:                 0.0, _storm_perspectives * _storm_rounds * _storm_per_round_usd
2526:             )
2527:             if _storm_cost_envelope > 0:
2528:                 _RUN_COST_CTX.set(current_run_cost() + _storm_cost_envelope)
2529:             # Raises BudgetExceededError (-> the sweep's abort_budget_exceeded handler) if the STORM
2530:             # envelope would breach the cap; STORM does NOT run in that case (the raise precedes the try).
2531:             _storm_check_budget(0)
2532:             # Codex diff-gate iter-3 P2: create the client AFTER the budget precheck so a clean
2533:             # envelope-breach abort does not leave an unclosed OpenRouterClient.
2534:             _storm_client = _StormClient(model=_STORM_MODEL)
2535:             _storm_ctx = _storm_cv.copy_context()
2536:             _storm_prev = _storm_mod.PG_STORM_ENABLED
2537:             _storm_mod.PG_STORM_ENABLED = True
2538:             try:
2539:                 _storm_out = await _storm_asyncio.create_task(
2540:                     _storm_mod.run_storm_interviews(_storm_client, _storm_state),
2541:                     context=_storm_ctx,
2542:                 )
2543:             except Exception as _storm_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — STORM faults (incl. its own internal
2544:                 # BudgetExceededError from the isolated context) never abort the run: the parent budget
2545:                 # was already booked + enforced via the envelope above.
2546:                 _storm_telemetry["firing_status"] = "error"
2547:                 _storm_telemetry["error"] = str(_storm_exc)[:200]
2548:                 _log(
2549:                     f"[storm]       STORM query expansion failed: {_storm_exc} — "
2550:                     f"proceeding without STORM queries"
2551:                 )
2552:             finally:
2553:                 _storm_mod.PG_STORM_ENABLED = _storm_prev
2554:                 try:
2555:                     await _storm_client.close()
2556:                 except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
2557:                     pass
2558:             _storm_questions = extract_storm_questions(
2559:                 _storm_out.get("storm_conversations", []),
2560:                 cap=int(os.getenv("PG_STORM_MAX_BENCHMARK_QUERIES", "30")),
2561:             )
2562:             if _storm_questions:
2563:                 _seen_lower = {x.lower() for x in _amplified_effective}
2564:                 _storm_added = [x for x in _storm_questions if x.lower() not in _seen_lower]
2565:                 _amplified_effective = _amplified_effective + _storm_added
2566:                 _storm_telemetry.update({
2567:                     "fired": len(_storm_added) > 0,
2568:                     "questions_added": len(_storm_added),
2569:                     "interviews": len(_storm_out.get("storm_conversations", [])),
2570:                     "firing_status": "fired" if _storm_added else "attempted_empty",
2571:                 })
2572:                 _log(
2573:                     f"[storm]       +{len(_storm_added)} perspective queries from "
2574:                     f"{len(_storm_out.get('storm_conversations', []))} interviews (slug={q['slug']})"
2575:                 )
2576: 
2577:         # I-meta-005 Phase 1 FIX 1 (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 #1): ON-mode
2578:         # computes NO template-keyed expander. The #817-L4 DOI-seed expander
2579:         # reads the domain scope template (None on-mode); it is gated on
2580:         # `if not _use_research_planner:` so no expander is computed on-mode
2581:         # (the on-path already passes `_retrieval_seed_urls = []`). OFF: runs
2582:         # byte-identically.
2583:         if not _use_research_planner:
2584:             # I-bug-776 (#817) layer-4 (Codex decision b): direct primary-trial DOI
2585:             # seed candidates. Search-expansion (M-35 above) does not surface the
2586:             # pivotal OA primaries for guideline-dominated questions, so inject the
2587:             # anchored trials' known DOIs as DIRECT candidates. Slug-scoped no-op
2588:             # when `per_query_primary_trial_dois` is absent for the slug.
2589:             from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.primary_trial_expander import (
2590:                 expand_primary_trial_dois,
2591:             )

2595:                      f"direct candidates (slug={q['slug']})")
2596:         else:
2597:             _trial_doi_seeds = []
2598: 
2599:         t0 = time.time()
2600:         # I-meta-005 Phase 1 (#985) + Phase 2 (#986): ON-mode bypasses the
2601:         # legacy domain router (brief §2.4) — `domain=None` skips the
2602:         # domain_backends per-domain `if domain ==` candidate router. Phase 2
2603:         # threads the planner FRAME so the field-agnostic NEED-TYPE registry
2604:         # (keyed on the frame's declared evidence_needs + jurisdictions, NO
2605:         # domain literal) REPLACES the domain backends at the live seam. The
2606:         # frame-derived protocol replaces the clinical PICO protocol so planner
2607:         # sub-queries validate against the frame's own tokens. No trial-DOI
2608:         # seeds on-mode. OFF: the legacy domain + PICO protocol + DOI seeds run
2609:         # byte-identically (research_frame=None -> the legacy `if domain ==`
2610:         # seam is taken).
2611:         _retrieval_domain = None if _use_research_planner else q["domain"]
2612:         _retrieval_protocol = (
2613:             _planner_protocol
2614:             if (_use_research_planner and _planner_protocol is not None)
2615:             else protocol
2616:         )
2617:         _retrieval_seed_urls = [] if _use_research_planner else _trial_doi_seeds
2618:         _retrieval_frame = (
2619:             _research_plan.frame
2620:             if (_use_research_planner and _research_plan is not None)
2621:             else None
2622:         )

2624:             research_question=q["question"],

2633:             seed_urls=_retrieval_seed_urls,   # #817 layer-4 DOI candidates (off-mode only)
2634:             research_frame=_retrieval_frame,  # Phase 2 need-type registry (None off-mode)
2635:         )

2638:              f"fetched={retrieval.candidates_fetched}, "

2640:              f"elapsed={dt:.1f}s  api_calls={retrieval.api_calls}")
2641:

2701:             )

2740:             except Exception: pass

2901:                      f"new evidence rows")

2903:                 existing_urls = {
2904:                     s.url for s in retrieval.classified_sources
2905:                 }
2906:                 for src in exp_retrieval.classified_sources:
2907:                     if src.url not in existing_urls:
2908:                         retrieval.classified_sources.append(src)
2909:                 existing_ev_ids = {
2910:                     ev["evidence_id"] for ev in retrieval.evidence_rows
2911:                 }
2912:                 # Renumber new evidence rows to avoid collisions
2913:                 base = len(retrieval.evidence_rows)
2914:                 for i, ev in enumerate(exp_retrieval.evidence_rows):
2915:                     new_id = f"ev_{base + i:03d}"
2916:                     ev["evidence_id"] = new_id
2917:                     retrieval.evidence_rows.append(ev)
2918:                 # Re-classify tier distribution with the merged corpus
2919:                 dist = compute_tier_distribution(
2920:                     retrieval.classified_sources, protocol,
2921:                 )
2922:                 tier_summary = ", ".join(
2923:                     f"{k}={v*100:.0f}%"
2924:                     for k, v in sorted(dist.tier_fractions.items())
2925:                 )
2926:                 # Re-check completeness
2927:                 completeness = check_completeness(
2928:                     domain=q["domain"],
2929:                     research_question=q["question"],
2930:                     evidence_rows=retrieval.evidence_rows,
2931:                 )
2932:                 _log(f"[expansion]   post: total={dist.total_sources} "
2933:                      f"covered={completeness.total_covered}/"
2934:                      f"{completeness.total_applicable}")
2935:                 # Also re-run adequacy
2936:                 adequacy = assess_corpus_adequacy(
2937:                     tier_counts=dist.tier_counts,
2938:                     evidence_row_count=len(retrieval.evidence_rows),
2939:                     domain=q["domain"],
2940:                     protocol=protocol,
2941:                 )
2942:                 (run_dir / "corpus_adequacy.json").write_text(
2943:                     json.dumps(asdict(adequacy), indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str)
2944:                     + "\n",
2945:                     encoding="utf-8",
2946:                 )
2947:             except Exception as exc:
2948:                 _log(f"[expansion]   FAILED: {exc}")
2949: 
2950:         # I-meta-002-q1d (#942-deepener): Stop-RAG-gated citation-snowball deepening. Default OFF (it

codex
I’m also checking row identity behavior. `run_live_retrieval` emits `evidence_id` as `ev_<post-fetch index>`, and several merge paths renumber appended rows. Any analysis sidecar should be built after the final corpus mutation it intends to describe, or it can join to stale evidence IDs.

3416:             summary["cost_usd"] = run_cost

3422:                 write_per_run_cost_ledger(run_dir, run_id)
3423:             except Exception:
3424:                 pass
3425:             if q.get("v6_mode") and q.get("external_run_id"):
3426:                 emit_terminal_event(
3427:                     q.get("external_run_id"),

3429:                     error_msg=summary.get("error"),

3468:                     for f in adequacy.findings if not f.ok
3469:                 )
3470:                 + "\n\n### Suggested next steps\n\n"
3471:                 "- Widen retrieval (more amplified queries, higher caps).\n"
3472:                 "- Relax adequacy thresholds if the domain has inherently "
3473:                 "sparse evidence.\n"
3474:                 "- Refine the research question to a narrower, better-"
3475:                 "supported sub-topic.\n",
3476:                 encoding="utf-8",
3477:             )
3478:             run_cost = current_run_cost()
3479:             # BUG-SCHEMA-R8d: use shared envelope so every exit path

3483:             )
3484:             manifest.update({

3630:         # Contradiction detection (now on the possibly-expanded evidence set)

3641:         # Merged into the SAME contradictions.json list (a `type:"qualitative"` discriminator +
3642:         # `severity:"review"` distinguish them; downstream renderers branch on those). Fail-open: a
3643:         # detector error never aborts the sweep.

3656:                         retrieval.evidence_rows, domain=q["domain"],
3657:                     )
3658:                 )
3659:         except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open, log loudly, never abort the sweep
3660:             _log(f"[qual-conflict] detector error (skipped, fail-open): {exc}")
3661:             qualitative_records = []
3662:         # Semantic/NLI cross-document contradiction detection (I-ready-012 #1079). The THIRD
3663:         # detector: an LLM-NLI pass catching prose-only directional contradictions with NO shared
3664:         # number and NO NegEx cue that the numeric+qualitative rule layers miss (the lethal-miss
3665:         # class). Default OFF (PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT) -> byte-identical when off (no judge built, no
3666:         # network). Fail-open: a detector/judge/import error logs + skips, never aborts the sweep; a
3667:         # BudgetExceededError is absorbed inside the detector as keep-partial (the run's hard cap is

3683:             semantic_records = []

3691:             encoding="utf-8",

3694:         _qual_review = sum(1 for qr in qualitative_records if qr.severity == "review")
3695:         _log(f"[contradict]  numeric_claims={len(numeric_claims)}  "

3699:         # Multi-section generation with Limitations (R-1)
3700:         # BUG-M-201 fix (deep-dive R6): tier-balanced + relevance-ranked
3701:         # selection instead of raw-order truncation. Previously the
3702:         # generator saw evidence_rows[:20] in retrieval order, diverging

3704:         from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_selector import (
3705:             select_evidence_for_generation,
3706:         )
3707:         max_ev = int(os.getenv("PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN", "20"))
3708:         # I-meta-005 Phase 5 (#989): finding-dedup + relevance-floor corpus
3709:         # (PG_USE_FINDING_DEDUP, default OFF). ON-mode replaces the max_ev cap with
3710:         # a relevance floor (keep every row >= floor, no cap) and dedups by finding
3711:         # before the generator. PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR default 0.30, range (0.0, 1.0];
3712:         # invalid/out-of-range fails LOUD (never silently send an unbounded pool).
3713:         _use_finding_dedup = (
3714:             os.getenv("PG_USE_FINDING_DEDUP", "0").strip()
3715:             in ("1", "true", "True")
3716:         )

3718:         # dedup-then-capped to PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN so #1070's cap holds at 1000 URLs (Codex brief
3719:         # P1-1). Default OFF (legacy no-cap floor mode unchanged); Gate-B turns it ON via the slate.

3722:             in ("1", "true", "True")
3723:         )

3731:             )

3734:         # primary papers. Anchors come from the loaded scope
3735:         # template's `per_query_primary_trial_anchors` map keyed by

3745:         # M-50 (2026-04-22): derive the T2D-direct anchor set from the
3746:         # template's population_scope labels. Anchors labeled "direct"
3747:         # qualify for per-trial subsections. SURMOUNT-1/3/4 (indirect
3748:         # for T2D) excluded so weight-loss-only trials don't generate
3749:         # T2D-misleading subsections.
3750:         def _m50_direct_anchors_for_sweep(tmpl, slug):
3751:             scope = get_trial_population_scope_for_slug(tmpl, slug)
3752:             direct = [a for a, lab in scope.items() if lab == "direct"]
3753:             if not direct and _primary_anchors:
3754:                 # When template has no scope labels, fall back to all
3755:                 # configured anchors (backwards-compatible with slugs
3756:                 # that haven't defined population_scope yet).
3757:                 direct = list(_primary_anchors)
3758:             return direct
3759: 
3760:         def _compute_m50_skip_anchors(
3761:             contract_plans: list[Any],
3762:             primary_anchors: list[str],
3763:         ) -> set[str]:
3764:             """Codex M-63 REJECT Medium 2: map contract entity ids
3765:             to M-50 trial anchor names via a normalized substring
3766:             match so the sweep can tell M-50 which anchors are
3767:             already owned by a contract slot.
3768: 
3769:             Normalization: strip `_`/`-`, lowercase. Match anchor
3770:             against each entity_id; a normalized-anchor substring
3771:             hit inside a normalized-entity-id is strong evidence
3772:             the contract slot renders that trial (e.g.
3773:             `surpass_2_primary` contains `surpass2` → SURPASS-2).
3774: 
3775:             Conservative: returns empty set if no contract plans
3776:             present, or if no match found. False negatives cost
3777:             one duplicate subsection (reader confusion, not
3778:             pipeline failure); false positives would SUPPRESS a
3779:             legitimate per-trial section, which is worse.
3780:             """
3781:             if not contract_plans or not primary_anchors:
3782:                 return set()
3783:             skip: set[str] = set()
3784:             for plan in contract_plans:
3785:                 slots = getattr(plan, "slots", ())
3786:                 for slot in slots:
3787:                     for eid in getattr(slot, "entity_ids", ()):
3788:                         norm_eid = (
3789:                             eid.lower().replace("_", "").replace("-", "")
3790:                         )
3791:                         for anchor in primary_anchors:
3792:                             norm_anchor = (
3793:                                 anchor.lower()
3794:                                 .replace("_", "").replace("-", "")
3795:                             )
3796:                             if norm_anchor and norm_anchor in norm_eid:
3797:                                 skip.add(anchor)
3798:                                 break
3799:             return skip
3800:         evidence_selection = select_evidence_for_generation(
3801:             research_question=q["question"],
3802:             protocol=protocol,
3803:             classified_sources=retrieval.classified_sources,
3804:             evidence_rows=retrieval.evidence_rows,
3805:             max_rows=max_ev,
3806:             primary_trial_anchors=_primary_anchors,
3807:             relevance_floor=_relevance_floor,   # Phase 5: None OFF -> 20-cap path
3808:         )
3809:         evidence_for_gen = evidence_selection.selected_rows
3810:         # I-ready-004 (#1078): CAPPED finding-dedup for Gate-B. The relevance-floor selection above is
3811:         # NO-CAP (keeps every row >= floor) — at 1000 URLs that bypasses #1070's PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN
3812:         # cap and floods the generator (Codex brief P1-1). When PG_CAPPED_FINDING_DEDUP is set, dedup the
3813:         # floored base by finding (so the cap's slots go to DISTINCT findings, not near-duplicates) THEN
3814:         # enforce the tier-balanced top-max_ev cap — so #1070's cap AND the relevance floor BOTH hold.
3815:         # REASSIGN evidence_selection to the capped object so manifest['evidence_selection'] reflects the
3816:         # actual generator base (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2-1). The contract/upload prepends below stay
3817:         # ADDITIVE (identical to OFF-mode: 150-cap base + additive prepends); the post-prepend dedup at
3818:         # ~L3790 still collapses any base<->prepend duplicate + emits the canonical finding_dedup
3819:         # telemetry. Default OFF outside Gate-B (legacy no-cap floor mode unchanged).
3820:         if _use_finding_dedup and _capped_dedup and _relevance_floor is not None:
3821:             _precap = len(evidence_for_gen)
3822:             evidence_selection = _capped_finding_dedup_selection(
3823:                 base_rows=evidence_for_gen,
3824:                 classified_sources=retrieval.classified_sources,
3825:                 research_question=q["question"],
3826:                 protocol=protocol,
3827:                 primary_trial_anchors=_primary_anchors,
3828:                 max_ev=max_ev,
3829:             )
3830:             evidence_for_gen = evidence_selection.selected_rows
3831:             _log(
3832:                 f"[capped-dedup] floored {_precap} -> dedup+capped {len(evidence_for_gen)} "
3833:                 f"(max_ev={max_ev}); #1070 cap + floor both hold"
3834:             )
3835:         # I-meta-005 Phase 4 (#988): snapshot the PRE-INJECTION selection baseline.
3836:         # Every non-selection injection below (V30 contract rows :2811, upload rows
3837:         # :2841) is a PREPEND, so this snapshot stays the contiguous SUFFIX of
3838:         # evidence_for_gen and `everything ahead of it` == the exact injected
3839:         # prepend a gap round must re-apply (Codex diff-gate P1).
3840:         _selection_base_rows = list(evidence_for_gen)
3841:         _log(f"[select]      strategy={evidence_selection.selection_strategy} "
3842:              f"selected={len(evidence_for_gen)} of {len(retrieval.evidence_rows)} "
3843:              f"dropped={evidence_selection.dropped_count}")
3844:         _log(f"              full_tiers={evidence_selection.full_counts} "
3845:              f"selected_tiers={evidence_selection.selected_counts}")
3846:         _log(f"[generation]  multi-section DeepSeek V3.2-Exp, "
3847:              f"evidence={len(evidence_for_gen)}...")
3848:         t0 = time.time()
3849:         # R-6 Gap-3: pass uncovered-topic labels so the Limitations
3850:         # paragraph can name the gaps explicitly.
3851:         uncovered_labels = [
3852:             next(
3853:                 (tc.topic.label for tc in completeness.topics
3854:                  if tc.topic.id == tid),

3859: 
3860:         # V30 Phase-2 M-63: when PG_V30_PHASE2_ENABLED=1, compile

4214:                 def _run_gap_round(gap_queries: list[str]) -> RoundOutcome:
4215:                     """On-mode gap-retrieval closure. Fires ONLY the gap
4216:                     sub-queries (anchor-suppressed BOTH seams), merges with
4217:                     global evidence_id renumber, re-selects, re-injects upload
4218:                     rows, and re-gates on the billed set. Closes over the
4219:                     enclosing `retrieval` / `evidence_for_gen` / `_suff` so the
4220:                     final round's state flows to the generator."""
4221:                     nonlocal retrieval, evidence_for_gen, _suff, _jo_sidecar
4222:                     _gap_ret = run_live_retrieval(

4236:                     # I-ready-017 #1134 (Codex diff-gate P1-1): a saturation gap
4237:                     # round re-retrieves AFTER the single source-filter, so its
4238:                     # rows must be filtered to citeable + their sidecar merged
4239:                     # here — otherwise unfiltered rows reach evidence_for_gen /
4240:                     # live_corpus, OR legitimate journal gap rows are false-aborted
4241:                     # by the pre-generator no-leak assert (no sidecar metadata).

4243:                         _jo_sidecar = _jof.merge_sidecars(

4247:                             _gap_ret.classified_sources, _jo_sidecar

4250:                             _gap_ret.evidence_rows, _jo_sidecar

5910:                 "plan_path": str((run_dir / "research_plan.json").name),
5911:                 "plan_sha256": _plan_sha,

5918:         # I-meta-005 Phase 4 (#988) — Codex diff-gate iter-2 P2a: surface the

5955:         # eval_gate already drove manifest['release_allowed'] + status above.
5956:         #
5957:         # When ON: D8 (apply_d8_release_policy, via sweep_integration) is the SINGLE binding
5958:         # gate. We OVERRIDE both manifest['release_allowed'] AND manifest['status'] from the
5959:         # D8 decision (so status and release_allowed cannot contradict — the double-gate the
5960:         # Codex P2 forbids) and DEMOTE the legacy evaluator_gate to advisory metadata only.
