5962:         # synthesizes claim_ids or a coverage denominator (fail-closed: sweep_integration
5963:         # raises on a blank id or an empty canonical required set).
5964:         _four_role_on = os.environ.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE", "0").strip() in (
5965:             "1", "true", "True",
5966:         )
5967:         # I-meta-008 (#1014) loud guard: PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE is on but NO transport was injected,
5968:         # so the 4-role seam stays INERT and this run would silently use the legacy single-evaluator
5969:         # gate. That is the exact "benchmark started via a legacy entrypoint" trap (#1014): the
5970:         # 4-role benchmark ONLY runs via scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py (its main()/the
5971:         # run_gate_b_query entrypoint INJECTS a transport).
5972:         # I-run11-009 (#1055): the loud log alone was NOT enough — the run still fell onto the legacy
5973:         # gate, whose own judge-unavailable fail-open could ship an unjudged report. A caller that
5974:         # explicitly asked for 4-role mode (PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE=1) but supplied no transport is
5975:         # MISCONFIGURED; it must FAIL CLOSED, never silently downgrade to the legacy gate (LAW II;
5976:         # §-1.1). Legacy callers that never set PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE are unaffected (the guard does not
5977:         # fire for them). Override the manifest built above to a hold.
5978:         if _four_role_on and four_role_transport is None:
5979:             print(
5980:                 "[I-meta-008][GUARD] PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE is ON but no four_role_transport was "
5981:                 "injected -- the 4-role benchmark seam is INERT. FAILING CLOSED (release HELD): "
5982:                 "this run will NOT silently fall back to the legacy single-evaluator gate. The "
5983:                 "native 4-role benchmark runs ONLY via scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py "
5984:                 "(CLI: python -m scripts.dr_benchmark.run_gate_b)."
5985:             )
5986:             # Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1: set the LOCAL summary_status/unified_status too, not just the
5987:             # manifest fields. The tail writes `summary["status"] = summary_status` (~L4813) and
5988:             # sweep_summary.{json,md} read that top-level status — without this a held misconfiguration
5989:             # could still surface as "ok"/"warn" in the summary while the manifest says held.
5990:             summary_status = "four_role_held"
5991:             unified_status = to_unified_status(summary_status)
5992:             manifest["release_allowed"] = False
5993:             manifest["status"] = unified_status
5994:             manifest["four_role_seam_inert"] = True
5995:         if _four_role_on and four_role_transport is not None:
5996:             # M3b: the seam resolves inputs (builder WINS over static four_role_inputs; both
5997:             # None -> fail-closed), runs the SINGLE binding D8 gate, and persists the per-claim
5998:             # audit map next to the run. The builder closure is called HERE — AFTER generation —
5999:             # so it sees the finished `multi` report; the sweep still synthesizes nothing itself.
6000:             from src.polaris_graph.roles.sweep_integration import (  # noqa: E402
6001:                 FourRoleEvaluationResult,
6002:                 build_evaluator_agrees_map,
6003:                 run_four_role_seam,
6004:             )
6005:             # I-meta-008 (#1028-followup / hang): the seam runs SYNCHRONOUS httpx verifier calls.
6006:             # Calling it directly in this async coroutine BLOCKS the event loop, and a wedged
6007:             # verifier call (per-call 900s x up to 3 attempts) could hang the whole run ~45 min with
6008:             # NO terminal artifact (run 5: 18-min poll() hang, no manifest.json). Run it on a worker
6009:             # thread bounded by an overall wall-clock timeout and FAIL CLOSED on timeout/error
6010:             # (release HELD — never release un-evaluated output, LAW II / §9.1). The timeout/error
6011:             # path returns a real HELD FourRoleEvaluationResult so the existing post-seam manifest
6012:             # block is byte-unchanged. Budget integrity: mirror the planner-LLM cost-reconciliation
6013:             # (this file ~L1840) — copy_context for READ visibility + write the worker's accumulated
6014:             # _RUN_COST_CTX delta back to the parent so PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN stays intact even though
6015:             # the seam ran in a copied ThreadPoolExecutor context.
6016:             import concurrent.futures as _seam_futures
6017:             import contextvars as _seam_cv
6018:             from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
6019:                 BudgetExceededError as _SeamBudgetExceededError,
6020:                 _RUN_COST_CTX as _seam_cost_ctx,
6021:             )
6022:             # I-run11-004: default raised 2400 -> 7200s. 2400 was the run-12 truncator — the
6023:             # 4-role seam (now incl. the reasoning-ON MiniMax-M2 decomposition Sentinel + the xhigh
6024:             # Mirror/Judge) takes minutes per claim, and a 2400s cap fired mid-run and held a
6025:             # truncated manifest. PG_VERIFIER_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (the per-call budget) stays 900.
6026:             # LAW VI: env-overridable.
6027:             _seam_timeout = float(
6028:                 os.environ.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "7200")
6029:             )
6030:             _seam_kg_path = out_root / "verified_claim_graph_campaign.db"
6031:             _seam_parent_cost = _seam_cost_ctx.get()
6032:             _seam_cost_holder = [_seam_parent_cost]
6033:             _seam_parent_ctx = _seam_cv.copy_context()
6034: 
6035:             # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1: narrow progress callback — keeps the heartbeat's last_update_utc
6036:             # advancing during the up-to-7200s verifier phase (the parent blocks on .result()).
6037:             # Plain closure threaded as an argument (NOT a ContextVar), so it is unaffected by the
6038:             # worker-thread context copy; _hb's atomic writer is concurrency-safe (unique temps).
6039:             # Codex AC1-gate P1-1: a 1-element "live" flag (matches the _seam_cost_holder idiom;
6040:             # GIL-atomic boolean read/write) so an ORPHANED worker that keeps running after a seam
6041:             # TIMEOUT (parent does shutdown(wait=False)) can NOT overwrite the terminal heartbeat or
6042:             # bleed a stale query's progress into the next query's state/run_status.json. The parent
6043:             # clears it in the seam finally below; the worker's callback then no-ops.
6044:             _seam_hb_live = [True]
6045: 
6046:             def _hb_claims(done: int, n: int) -> None:
6047:                 # Codex AC1-gate iter-2 P1-1: check the live flag AND write the heartbeat under the
6048:                 # SAME lock the parent holds when it clears the flag, so an entered-but-preempted
6049:                 # worker callback can NOT land a stale four_role_progress after the parent has cleared
6050:                 # the flag and written terminal status (the check-then-write race in iter-1's guard).
6051:                 with _hb_lock:
6052:                     if not _seam_hb_live[0]:
6053:                         return
6054:                     _hb("four_role_progress", claims_verified=done, claims_total=n)
6055: 
6056:             def _seam_worker() -> FourRoleEvaluationResult:
6057:                 def _run_under_ctx() -> FourRoleEvaluationResult:
6058:                     try:
6059:                         return run_four_role_seam(
6060:                             four_role_transport,
6061:                             run_dir=run_dir,
6062:                             timestamp=_utc_now_iso(),
6063:                             four_role_input_builder=four_role_input_builder,
6064:                             four_role_inputs=four_role_inputs,
6065:                             multi=multi,
6066:                             template=_template,
6067:                             slug=q["slug"],
6068:                             domain=q["domain"],
6069:                             ev_pool=ev_pool,
6070:                             # I-meta-002-q1d (#948): persist the snowball KG to the CAMPAIGN-scoped
6071:                             # db so later questions in this sweep can reuse THIS question's VERIFIED
6072:                             # claims (fail-closed read).
6073:                             campaign_kg_db=str(_seam_kg_path),
6074:                             # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1: progress callback (anti-staleness during the
6075:                             # up-to-7200s verifier phase). Opaque to sweep_integration.
6076:                             heartbeat_claims_cb=_hb_claims,
6077:                         )
6078:                     finally:
6079:                         # Capture accumulated verifier spend even on raise/timeout so the parent
6080:                         # write-back below cannot under-report (budget-cap integrity, LAW II).
6081:                         _seam_cost_holder[0] = _seam_cost_ctx.get()
6082:                 return _seam_parent_ctx.run(_run_under_ctx)
6083: 
6084:             _seam_held_reason = None
6085:             # Manage the executor MANUALLY (NOT `with`): a `with ThreadPoolExecutor` __exit__ calls
6086:             # shutdown(wait=True), which BLOCKS until the wedged worker finishes — defeating the
6087:             # timeout entirely (Codex diff-gate P1). shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True) returns
6088:             # promptly so the held manifest is written AT PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS; the
6089:             # orphaned worker thread exits on its own per-call timeout. Mirrors audit_ir/parallel_fetch.py.
6090:             _hb("four_role_started")  # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1: bracket the up-to-7200s verifier phase
6091:             _seam_pool = _seam_futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
6092:             try:
6093:                 four_role_result = _seam_pool.submit(_seam_worker).result(
6094:                     timeout=_seam_timeout
6095:                 )
6096:             except _seam_futures.TimeoutError:
6097:                 _seam_held_reason = "seam_timeout"
6098:             except _SeamBudgetExceededError:
6099:                 # A verifier cap breach (PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN) MUST propagate to the existing outer
6100:                 # abort_budget_exceeded handler (the clean budget-abort contract) — NOT be swallowed
6101:                 # into a held seam_error (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1). The worker's `finally` already
6102:                 # updated the cost holder, so the `finally` below reconciles the full spend before
6103:                 # this re-raises.
6104:                 raise
6105:             except Exception as _seam_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - any OTHER seam failure must fail CLOSED
6106:                 _seam_held_reason = f"seam_error:{type(_seam_exc).__name__}:{str(_seam_exc)[:120]}"
6107:             finally:
6108:                 # Codex AC1-gate iter-2 P1-1: clear the live flag UNDER _hb_lock so it is mutually
6109:                 # exclusive with the worker's check-then-write in _hb_claims. An in-flight callback
6110:                 # either completes its write BEFORE this acquires the lock, or is blocked and then sees
6111:                 # the cleared flag and returns — so NO four_role_progress write can land after this
6112:                 # point. Stops an orphaned post-timeout worker from overwriting the terminal heartbeat
6113:                 # or bleeding stale progress into the next query's state/run_status.json.
6114:                 with _hb_lock:
6115:                     _seam_hb_live[0] = False
6116:                 # NON-BLOCKING shutdown so a wedged worker cannot delay the held manifest (P1).
6117:                 _seam_pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
6118:                 # Budget reconciliation: on the SUCCESS path the worker's `finally` already updated
6119:                 # `_seam_cost_holder`, so this write-back is EXACT. On the TIMEOUT path the worker is
6120:                 # still running (holder == parent cost -> delta 0), so the in-flight verifier spend is
6121:                 # NOT added to the parent cap — an acceptable tradeoff (Codex P2): the run is ABORTING
6122:                 # (release HELD), the operator authorized spend, the orphaned worker's cost is bounded
6123:                 # by its own per-call timeout, and prompt fail-closed termination outranks exact
6124:                 # budget accounting on an already-aborted run.
6125:                 _seam_delta = _seam_cost_holder[0] - _seam_parent_cost
6126:                 if _seam_delta > 0:
6127:                     _seam_cost_ctx.set(_seam_parent_cost + _seam_delta)
6128:             if _seam_held_reason is not None:
6129:                 _log(
6130:                     f"[four_role]   SEAM {_seam_held_reason} after <= {_seam_timeout}s "
6131:                     "-> release HELD (fail-closed; terminal manifest written)"
6132:                 )
6133:                 four_role_result = FourRoleEvaluationResult(
6134:                     release_allowed=False,
6135:                     held_reasons=[_seam_held_reason],
6136:                     gaps=[],
6137:                     final_verdicts={},
6138:                     records=[],
6139:                     coverage_fraction=0.0,
6140:                     fabricated_occurrence_latched=False,
6141:                     needs_rewrite=[],
6142:                     kg_path=_seam_kg_path,
6143:                 )
6144:             # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1: verifier phase complete — stamp claims processed.
6145:             try:
6146:                 _n_claims = len(getattr(four_role_result, "final_verdicts", {}) or {})
6147:                 _hb("four_role_done", claims_verified=_n_claims, claims_total=_n_claims)
6148:             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
6149:                 pass
6150:             # Demote the legacy gate to ADVISORY metadata; D8 owns the headline decision.
6151:             manifest["evaluator_gate_advisory"] = manifest.pop("evaluator_gate")
6152:             manifest["release_allowed"] = four_role_result.release_allowed
6153:             # Single binding status: released => success; held => release-blocking abort.
6154:             summary_status = (
6155:                 "four_role_released"
6156:                 if four_role_result.release_allowed
6157:                 else "four_role_held"
6158:             )
6159:             # Reassign BOTH the summary label AND the unified local so manifest.json,
6160:             # sweep_summary.json (summary["status"] at the function tail), and the status log
6161:             # line are all D8-driven and cannot disagree (no double-gate, Codex P2).
6162:             unified_status = to_unified_status(summary_status)
6163:             manifest["status"] = unified_status
6164:             # final_verdicts (keyed by EXISTING claim_id) drive evaluator_agrees at the real
6165:             # assembly point (clinical_generator, Gate-B); they are surfaced here so the D8
6166:             # decision is fully auditable from the manifest. evaluator_agrees_from_verdict maps
6167:             # VERIFIED->True / else->False (the helper lives in sweep_integration). The sweep's
6168:             # SectionResult path holds SentenceVerification, NOT VerifiedSentence, so there is no
6169:             # VerifiedSentence object to write here — populating one would be fake wiring.
6170:             manifest["four_role_evaluation"] = {
6171:                 "release_allowed": four_role_result.release_allowed,
6172:                 "held_reasons": four_role_result.held_reasons,
6173:                 "coverage_fraction": round(four_role_result.coverage_fraction, 3),
6174:                 "fabricated_occurrence_latched": (

6176:                 ),
6177:                 "needs_rewrite": four_role_result.needs_rewrite,

6179:                 "gaps": [

6185:                     }
6186:                     for gap in four_role_result.gaps

6190:             # I-meta-002 PR-9/M5: ADDITIVE per-claim evaluator_agrees MAP for audit/inspector

5036:                 # block), so when the planner path did NOT run, the closure hit an UnboundLocalError
5037:                 # ("cannot access free variable PG_GENERATOR_MODEL") and the Phase-7 quantified
5038:                 # differentiator silently no-op'd (run 5: spec_produced=False). Binding it in this
5039:                 # always-executed (PG_ENABLE_QUANTIFIED_ANALYSIS=1) block fixes that.

5049: 
5050:                 async def _q_spec_provider(_question, _sourced):
5051:                     # The ONLY billed step: ask the Writer for a JSON ModelSpec
5052:                     # over the EXISTING extracted sourced numbers; parse defensively.
5053:                     _shortlist = [
5054:                         {"evidence_id": d.get("evidence_id"), "label": d.get("label"),
5055:                          "context": d.get("context"), "value": d.get("value"),
5056:                          "unit": d.get("unit")}
5057:                         for d in _sourced[:40]
5058:                     ]
5059:                     _prompt = (
5060:                         "You are modeling a quantified trade-off for a research "
5061:                         "report. Using ONLY the sourced numbers below, emit a "
5062:                         "SINGLE JSON object (no prose) with keys model_id, title, "
5063:                         "inputs, outputs, sensitivity, solve_for per the POLARIS "
5064:                         "ModelSpec schema. Each SOURCED input MUST carry "
5065:                         "datapoint_ref:{ev_id,label,context,value,unit} copied "
5066:                         "EXACTLY from one listed number; mark every ASSUMPTION "
5067:                         "input modeled:true with base+unit+sweep. Every output "
5068:                         "formula must be pure arithmetic over the declared input "
5069:                         "names. If the numbers do not support a defensible model, "
5070:                         'return {"model_id":"none"} and nothing else.\n\n'
5071:                         f"QUESTION: {_question}\n\n"
5072:                         f"SOURCED NUMBERS (JSON): {_q_json.dumps(_shortlist)[:8000]}"
5073:                     )
5074:                     _client = OpenRouterClient(model=PG_GENERATOR_MODEL)
5075:                     try:
5076:                         _resp = await _client.generate(
5077:                             _prompt, max_tokens=1500, temperature=0.0,
5078:                         )
5079:                     finally:
5080:                         await _client.close()
5081:                     _txt = getattr(_resp, "content", "") or ""
5082:                     _m = _q_re.search(r"\{.*\}", _txt, _q_re.DOTALL)
5083:                     if not _m:
5084:                         return None
5085:                     try:
5086:                         _obj = _q_json.loads(_m.group(0))
5087:                     except _q_json.JSONDecodeError:
5088:                         return None
5089:                     if (not isinstance(_obj, dict)
5090:                             or _obj.get("model_id") in (None, "", "none")):
5091:                         return None
5092:                     return _obj
5093:

5518:                     reason_counts[key] = reason_counts.get(key, 0) + 1
5519:         verif_details["drop_reason_counts"] = reason_counts
5520:         # I-ready-002 (#1071) P0: surface the BINDING-verifier judge_error RATE + flag a degraded run.
5521:         # Each judge_error already FAILED CLOSED (its sentence was dropped, reason
5522:         # entailment_judge_error_fail_closed) so no unverified claim shipped; this is the run-level
5523:         # guard + observability Codex's plan-gate required. denominator = all sentences the verifier
5524:         # actually checked (kept + dropped) across sections.
5525:         # FX-09 (I-ready-017): denominator = ACTUAL judge invocations this run (delta of
5526:         # the process-lifetime telemetry counter), NOT all verifier-checked sentences.
5527:         # The judge only runs on sentences that pass every mechanical check first, so the
5528:         # old kept+dropped denominator diluted the #1071 binding abort_verifier_degraded
5529:         # gate ~2.87x. Numerator = telemetry['judge_error'] delta (counts errors even on
5530:         # KEPT sentences that failed open, which the reason_counts grep under-counts).
5531:         _verifier_sentences_checked = sum(
5532:             len(s.get("kept", [])) + len(s.get("dropped", []))
5533:             for s in verif_details["sections"]
5534:         )
5535:         if _run_judge_tel is not None:
5536:             # Per-run, concurrency-isolated counter (started at the run boundary). Read
5537:             # directly via the pure delta helper with a zero base (the per-run dict
5538:             # starts at 0), so the denominator is THIS run's judge calls only.
5539:             _judge_calls, _judge_err = _judge_calls_and_errors_from_telemetry(
5540:                 {"calls": 0, "judge_error": 0}, _run_judge_tel,
5541:             )
5542:         else:
5543:             # Telemetry unavailable (defensive): fall back to the old reason-grep rate so
5544:             # the degraded gate still functions (fail-functional, never silently inert).
5545:             _judge_err = reason_counts.get("entailment_judge_error_fail_closed", 0)
5546:             _judge_calls = _verifier_sentences_checked
5547:         _judge_err_rate = (_judge_err / _judge_calls) if _judge_calls else 0.0
5548:         _max_jerr = float(os.getenv("PG_MAX_JUDGE_ERROR_RATE", "1.0"))
5549:         verif_details["judge_error_count"] = _judge_err
5550:         verif_details["judge_calls"] = _judge_calls
5551:         # Kept+dropped count retained as CONTEXT only (NOT the denominator).
5552:         verif_details["verifier_sentences_checked"] = _verifier_sentences_checked
5553:         verif_details["judge_error_sentences_checked"] = _verifier_sentences_checked  # back-compat alias
5554:         verif_details["judge_error_rate"] = round(_judge_err_rate, 4)
5555:         verif_details["judge_error_rate_cap"] = _max_jerr
5556:         verif_details["judge_error_degraded"] = _judge_err_rate > _max_jerr
5557:         if _judge_err_rate > _max_jerr:
5558:             _log(
5559:                 f"[verifier]    DEGRADED: judge_error_rate={_judge_err_rate:.3f} "
5560:                 f"({_judge_err}/{_judge_calls} judge calls) > cap {_max_jerr:.3f} — the binding entailment "
5561:                 f"verifier errored on too many sentences; the run is NOT trustworthy (each errored "
5562:                 f"sentence was dropped, but the rate indicates a degraded judge)."
5563:             )
5564:         elif _judge_err > 0:
5565:             _log(f"[verifier]    judge_error_rate={_judge_err_rate:.3f} ({_judge_err}/{_judge_calls} judge calls, under cap {_max_jerr:.3f})")
5566:         # I-gen-005 Step 1.5: tally each post-strict-verify category
5567:         # separately so the operator can distinguish the three drop
5568:         # paths (strict_verify failures, dedup consolidations, M-41c
5569:         # policy drops).

5572:             for s in verif_details["sections"]
5573:         )

5575:             len(s.get("dropped_by_m41c_underframed", []))
5576:             for s in verif_details["sections"]
5577:         )
5578:         (run_dir / "verification_details.json").write_text(
5579:             json.dumps(verif_details, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
5580:             encoding="utf-8",
5581:         )

1814: def strict_verify(
1815:     draft_text: str,
1816:     evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1817:     *,
1818:     require_number_match: bool = True,
1819:     telemetry_block: str | None = None,
1820:     quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None = None,
1821: ) -> StrictVerificationReport:
1822:     """Run strict verification on a draft. Drops failing sentences.
1823: 
1824:     Gap-3 (original): the Limitations paragraph was pass-through because
1825:     it discusses pipeline telemetry, not evidence.
1826: 
1827:     BUG-M-204 fix (deep-dive R10): when `telemetry_block` is supplied,
1828:     Limitations sentences ARE verified against it — every numeric
1829:     claim in the sentence must appear verbatim in the telemetry block.
1830:     This catches a fabricated "only 3% of sources are T1" claim when
1831:     the telemetry actually says "T1: 9%". Backward-compatible: if
1832:     telemetry_block is None (default), falls back to pass-through.
1833:     """
1834:     findings_text, limitations_text = split_findings_and_limitations(draft_text)
1835: 
1836:     kept: list[SentenceVerification] = []
1837:     dropped: list[SentenceVerification] = []
1838: 
1839:     # Findings: strict provenance verification
1840:     findings_sentences = split_into_sentences(findings_text)
1841:     for s in findings_sentences:
1842:         v = verify_sentence_provenance(
1843:             s, evidence_pool,
1844:             require_number_match=require_number_match,
1845:             quantified_models=quantified_models,
1846:         )
1847:         if v.is_verified:
1848:             kept.append(v)
1849:         else:
1850:             dropped.append(v)
1851: 
1852:     # Limitations: telemetry-grounded verification if block supplied,
1853:     # else pass-through (M-204 backward-compat).
1854:     limitations_sentences = split_into_sentences(limitations_text)
1855:     for s in limitations_sentences:
1856:         if telemetry_block is not None:
1857:             v = verify_limitations_sentence_against_telemetry(
1858:                 s, telemetry_block,
1859:             )
1860:             if v.is_verified:
1861:                 kept.append(v)
1862:             else:
1863:                 dropped.append(v)
1864:         else:
1865:             tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(s)
1866:             kept.append(SentenceVerification(
1867:                 sentence=s,
1868:                 tokens=tokens,
1869:                 is_verified=True,
1870:                 failure_reasons=[],
1871:                 soft_warnings=["limitations_paragraph_pass_through"],
1872:             ))
1873: 
1874:     total_in = len(findings_sentences) + len(limitations_sentences)
1875:     return StrictVerificationReport(
1876:         kept_sentences=kept,
1877:         dropped_sentences=dropped,
1878:         total_in=total_in,
1879:         total_kept=len(kept),
1880:         total_dropped=len(dropped),
1881:     )
1882:

1885: # Resolution: strip provenance tokens, add citation markers
1886: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1888: 
1889: def resolve_provenance_to_citations(
1890:     kept_sentences: list[SentenceVerification],
1891:     evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1892: ) -> tuple[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]:
1893:     """Strip [#ev:...] tokens and replace with numbered citations.
1894: 
1895:     Returns (rendered_text, bibliography_list). Bibliography is a list
1896:     of dicts: {num, evidence_id, url, tier, statement}.
1897:     """
1898:     ev_to_num: dict[str, int] = {}
1899:     biblio: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
1900: 
1901:     def _num_for(ev_id: str) -> int:
1902:         if ev_id not in ev_to_num:
1903:             ev_to_num[ev_id] = len(ev_to_num) + 1
1904:             ev = evidence_pool.get(ev_id, {})
1905:             biblio.append({
1906:                 "num": ev_to_num[ev_id],
1907:                 "evidence_id": ev_id,
1908:                 "url": ev.get("source_url", ""),
1909:                 "tier": ev.get("tier", ""),
1910:                 "statement": (ev.get("statement") or "")[:300],
1911:             })
1912:         return ev_to_num[ev_id]
1913: 
1914:     findings_lines: list[str] = []
1915:     limitations_lines: list[str] = []
1916:     for sv in kept_sentences:
1917:         # Strip provenance tokens first so degenerate fragments can be
1918:         # detected before we assign citation numbers (otherwise the
1919:         # bibliography keeps an entry whose only citing sentence we
1920:         # later drop). `stripped` is the FINAL rendered sentence body
1921:         # — must PRESERVE atom_NNN so PR #906 Step 3b validator can
1922:         # see atom citations downstream in verified_text.
1923:         stripped = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sv.sentence).strip()
1924:         # Phase 7 (#991): strip calc tokens too so a verified computed-number
1925:         # sentence carries its source-input [N] citations but no token leak.
1926:         stripped = _CALC_TOKEN_RE.sub("", stripped).strip()
1927:         # Clean trailing spaces before punctuation
1928:         stripped = re.sub(r"\s+([.!?,;])", r"\1", stripped)
1929:         # BUG-M-8 (Codex pass 9): drop degenerate sentence fragments
1930:         # that survive strict_verify as bare punctuation + citation
1931:         # (observed in the Novo sweep as ".[4]", "Morgan analysts.[12]",
1932:         # ".[14]" between legitimate sentences). A real sentence has
1933:         # ≥3 content words AND ≥15 chars of prose after provenance
1934:         # stripping. Lower bounds deliberately conservative — the
1935:         # shortest legitimate research sentences in smoke runs
1936:         # ("No contradictions detected.") comfortably clear it.
1937:         # Step 3b commit 1 follow-up iter-2 (Codex PR #906 iter-2 P1):
1938:         # use _verifier_cleaned_text ONLY for the word/length count
1939:         # (so "atom" in "atom_003" does not inflate the count) — but
1940:         # DO NOT modify the rendered `stripped` itself; the downstream
1941:         # validator must see atom_NNN tokens in verified_text.
1942:         _for_count = _verifier_cleaned_text(sv.sentence)
1943:         _content_w = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z]+", _for_count)
1944:         if len(_content_w) < 3 or len(_for_count) < 15:
1945:             continue
1946:         # Assign citation numbers only for surviving sentences
1947:         used_nums: list[int] = []
1948:         for tok in sv.tokens:
1949:             n = _num_for(tok.evidence_id)
1950:             if n not in used_nums:
1951:                 used_nums.append(n)
1952:         # Append citation markers
1953:         markers = "".join(f"[{n}]" for n in used_nums)
1954:         sentence_out = stripped + markers
1955:

406: @dataclass
407: class ProvenanceToken:
408:     evidence_id: str
409:     start: int
410:     end: int
411:     raw: str
412: 
413:     @property
414:     def span_len(self) -> int:
415:         return self.end - self.start

418: @dataclass
419: class SentenceVerification:
420:     sentence: str
421:     tokens: list[ProvenanceToken]
422:     is_verified: bool
423:     failure_reasons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
424:     resolved_citation_marker: str = ""   # e.g., "[1]"
425:     # Gap-2: soft warnings that don't trigger a drop but get surfaced
426:     # to the evaluator (stored as PT13 in external_evaluator).
427:     soft_warnings: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
428:     # Phase 0b Delta 3 (I-meta-005, gap-#18): True when the entailment judge
429:     # failed OPEN ((ENTAILED,"judge_error: ...")). Additive, default False —
430:     # inert in off mode (is_verified unchanged). ON mode (enforce) reads this
431:     # to fail-closed. OFF byte-identity is defined over behavioral/output
432:     # fields + rendered artifacts, NOT raw dataclass asdict (Codex iter-3 P2).
433:     judge_error: bool = False
434:     # I-cred-001 (Phase 1, L7) — per-claim CREDIBILITY DISCLOSURE side-outputs. Default-OFF inert
435:     # plumbing: these are NEVER inputs to is_verified / the six strict_verify checks; they are
436:     # populated + rendered only when the credibility-disclosure flag is ON (Phase 8), so OFF behaviour
437:     # and the rendered report stay byte-identical. Additive, exactly like soft_warnings / judge_error.
438:     span_verdict: str = ""                       # explicit per-claim verdict, e.g. "SUPPORTS" (not "EXISTS")
439:     credibility_weight: float | None = None      # the source's disclosed credibility weight
440:     independent_origin_count: int | None = None  # "N sources -> M independent origins" (post-collapse)
441:     certainty_label: str = ""                    # e.g. "high" / "moderate" / "low"
442: 
443: 
444: def parse_provenance_tokens(sentence: str) -> list[ProvenanceToken]:
445:     """Extract all [#ev:id:start-end] tokens from a sentence."""
446:     return [
447:         ProvenanceToken(
448:             evidence_id=m.group("ev_id"),
449:             start=int(m.group("start")),
450:             end=int(m.group("end")),
451:             raw=m.group(0),
452:         )
453:         for m in _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.finditer(sentence)
454:     ]

461: _NUMBER_RE = re.compile(r"-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
462: 
463: # Decimal-only pattern (e.g., 14.9, 3.4, 44.2). Integers like "STEP 1",
464: # "week 68", "104 weeks" are study/duration markers, not the claim
465: # itself — so we don't require them to appear in the provenance span.

codex
The provenance dataclass already has the four P8 disclosure fields, and `resolve_provenance_to_citations` currently ignores them. So rendering there is structurally additive, but the hook has to happen before `resolve_provenance_to_citations` is called inside `multi_section_generator` or else `verified_text` has already been frozen.

codex
The committed modules are already default-off at their own level, but that is not enough for the live path. The integration needs one top-level activation flag that prevents importing/calling the whole chain when off; otherwise per-module flag defaults are easy to miscompose into partial-on behavior.

 succeeded in 2117ms:
1: """I-cred-002 (Phase 2, L1 / §9.1) — adaptive LLM credibility skill (reliability × relevance).
2: 
3: ONE generic, domain-agnostic credibility skill. Per research question it scores EACH candidate
4: source on RELIABILITY × RELEVANCE with a written, inspectable rationale, consuming POLARIS's
5: already-computed deterministic authority signals (``authority_score`` / ``source_class`` /
6: ``corroboration_count`` / ``authority_confidence`` / ``signal_scores`` / ``junk_class`` /
7: ``predatory_oa``) as its PRIORS. There are NO fixed domain rubrics: the detected ``domain`` is a
8: single HINT field the judge reasons over, never a branch that swaps rubrics — so this scales to any
9: field (operator directive 2026-06-08, plan §9.1).
10: 
11: FAITHFULNESS POSTURE (binding):
12:   * ADVISORY ONLY. This never becomes a faithfulness gate — ``strict_verify``'s six per-sentence
13:     checks (``generator/provenance_generator.py``) remain the ONLY binding gate. A credibility
14:     weight/rationale is a side-output to disclose, never a reason to keep or drop a sentence.
15:   * DEFAULT-OFF byte-identical: ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_SKILL`` (no production caller is added here).
16:   * The LLM call is DEPENDENCY-INJECTED (``judge``); with no judge → no network, no client, no spend
17:     (mirrors ``retrieval/semantic_conflict_detector``). The production judge is wired by the caller,
18:     never constructed in this pure library.
19:   * NO row mutation, NO faithfulness-file import. Pure functions, snake_case, explicit imports.
20:   * LAW VI: every threshold is an env-overridable named constant (no magic numbers, no hardcoded
21:     model/endpoint).
22: 
23: ANTI-FABRICATION (the LOW/thin guardrail): for a LOW-confidence or thin-signal source the judge's
24: reliability is capped at ``clamp01(authority_score) + PG_CREDIBILITY_MAX_UPLIFT`` — the model cannot
25: invent authority that the deterministic signals do not support. The judge MAY freely DOWN-rate any
26: source below its prior (a high-authority but irrelevant/weak source can score low); the prior is NOT
27: a lower bound. The deterministic priors-only judgment is the fallback ONLY on a judge error / no
28: judge, never a universal floor.
29: """

61: def credibility_skill_enabled() -> bool:
62:     """True unless ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_SKILL`` is unset/falsey (default OFF => byte-identical).
63: 
64:     Caller kill-switch: no production caller invokes this library while OFF, so the rendered report
65:     + manifest are unchanged. The pure functions below do NOT read the flag — they are total +
66:     offline-testable; the caller gates invocation.
67:     """
68:     return os.environ.get(_FLAG, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
69: 
70: 
71: def _float_env(name: str, default: float) -> float:
72:     try:
73:         return float(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)
74:     except (TypeError, ValueError):
75:         return default
76: 
77: 
78: def _int_env(name: str, default: int) -> int:
79:     try:
80:         return int(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)
81:     except (TypeError, ValueError):
82:         return default
83: 
84: 
85: def _clamp01(value: Any) -> float | None:
86:     """Clamp a numeric to [0, 1]. Returns ``None`` for non-numeric / NaN / inf (the caller treats
87:     that as a judge error — never a NaN weight, never a crash)."""
88:     try:
89:         x = float(value)
90:     except (TypeError, ValueError):
91:         return None
92:     if math.isnan(x) or math.isinf(x):
93:         return None
94:     return 0.0 if x < 0.0 else 1.0 if x > 1.0 else x
95: 
96: 
97: @dataclass
98: class CredibilityJudgment:
99:     """One source's advisory credibility judgment (a side-output, never a verdict input).
100: 
101:     ``reliability_score`` is AFTER the anti-fabrication cap; ``relevance_score`` is the judged
102:     directness to the question (unknown => 1.0, multiplicative-neutral); ``credibility_weight`` is
103:     the FIXED product ``clamp01(reliability * relevance)``. ``signals_cited`` is the subset of the
104:     deterministic prior signals the judge reasoned from. ``judge_error`` is True iff the injected
105:     judge raised / returned malformed output for THIS source (isolated; priors-only fallback used).
106:     """
107: 
108:     evidence_id: str
109:     reliability_score: float
110:     relevance_score: float
111:     credibility_weight: float
112:     rationale: str
113:     signals_cited: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
114:     query_need: str = ""
115:     judge_error: bool = False
116: 
117: 
118: def _present_signals(row: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
119:     """The deterministic prior signals actually present (non-empty) on this row."""
120:     return [k for k in _PRIOR_SIGNAL_KEYS if row.get(k) not in (None, "", {}, [])]
121: 
122: 
123: def _row_snippet(row: dict[str, Any], max_chars: int) -> str:
124:     text = str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or row.get("text") or "")
125:     return text[:max_chars]
126: 
127: 
128: def _build_judge_payload(
129:     research_question: str, row: dict[str, Any], domain: str | None
130: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
131:     """Pure: assemble the judge payload for ONE source. Does NOT mutate ``row``.
132: 
133:     Carries source identity + bounded descriptors (title / url / snippet — so RELEVANCE is actually
134:     judgeable) + the deterministic authority priors + the ``domain_hint`` (a single string field,
135:     NOT a branch / rubric table). The judge sees ONLY this payload.
136:     """
137:     snippet_chars = _int_env(_ENV_SNIPPET_CHARS, _DEFAULT_SNIPPET_CHARS)
138:     return {
139:         "research_question": research_question,
140:         "evidence_id": str(row.get("evidence_id", "")),
141:         "title": str(row.get("title", "") or ""),
142:         "url": str(row.get("source_url", "") or row.get("url", "") or ""),
143:         "snippet": _row_snippet(row, snippet_chars),
144:         "authority_score": row.get("authority_score"),
145:         "source_class": row.get("source_class"),
146:         "corroboration_count": row.get("corroboration_count"),
147:         "authority_confidence": row.get("authority_confidence"),
148:         "signal_scores": dict(row.get("signal_scores") or {}),
149:         "junk_class": row.get("junk_class", ""),
150:         "predatory_oa": row.get("predatory_oa", False),
151:         "origin_cluster_id": row.get("origin_cluster_id"),  # Phase-4 independence signal (None pre-wiring)
152:         "domain_hint": domain or "",
153:     }
154: 
155: 
156: def _is_low_or_thin(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
157:     """A source is LOW/thin (subject to the anti-fabrication cap) when its authority_confidence is
158:     LOW, or it has no authority_score, or it has no signal_scores."""
159:     if str(row.get("authority_confidence") or "").strip().upper() == "LOW":
160:         return True
161:     if row.get("authority_score") is None:
162:         return True
163:     if not (row.get("signal_scores") or {}):
164:         return True
165:     return False
166: 
167: 
168: def _priors_only_judgment(row: dict[str, Any]) -> CredibilityJudgment:
169:     """The deterministic fallback (no judge / judge error): reliability from the prior, relevance
170:     neutral (1.0). Never fabricates relevance it cannot judge."""
171:     auth = _clamp01(row.get("authority_score"))
172:     reliability = auth if auth is not None else 0.0
173:     return CredibilityJudgment(
174:         evidence_id=str(row.get("evidence_id", "")),
175:         reliability_score=reliability,
176:         relevance_score=1.0,
177:         credibility_weight=reliability,  # == clamp01(reliability * 1.0)
178:         rationale="no judge wired — deterministic priors only",
179:         signals_cited=_present_signals(row),
180:         query_need="",
181:         judge_error=False,
182:     )
183: 
184: 
185: def _apply_judge(
186:     research_question: str,
187:     row: dict[str, Any],
188:     domain: str | None,
189:     judge: Callable[[str, dict], dict],
190: ) -> CredibilityJudgment:
191:     """Run the injected judge for ONE source; fall back to priors on ANY error / malformed output
192:     (isolated to this row — recall-first, fail-loud-but-bounded)."""
193:     payload = _build_judge_payload(research_question, row, domain)
194:     try:
195:         raw = judge(research_question, payload)
196:     except Exception:
197:         fallback = _priors_only_judgment(row)
198:         fallback.judge_error = True
199:         return fallback
200:     if not isinstance(raw, dict):
201:         fallback = _priors_only_judgment(row)
202:         fallback.judge_error = True
203:         return fallback
204: 
205:     reliability = _clamp01(raw.get("reliability_score"))
206:     if reliability is None:  # missing / NaN / inf reliability => judge error for this row
207:         fallback = _priors_only_judgment(row)
208:         fallback.judge_error = True
209:         return fallback
210:     relevance = _clamp01(raw.get("relevance_score"))
211:     if relevance is None:  # unknown / malformed relevance => multiplicative-neutral
212:         relevance = 1.0
213: 
214:     # Anti-fabrication cap: a LOW/thin source's reliability cannot exceed prior + max_uplift.
215:     if _is_low_or_thin(row):
216:         auth = _clamp01(row.get("authority_score")) or 0.0
217:         cap = _clamp01(auth + _float_env(_ENV_MAX_UPLIFT, _DEFAULT_MAX_UPLIFT))
218:         if cap is not None:
219:             reliability = min(reliability, cap)
220: 
221:     present = set(_present_signals(row))
222:     cited = [s for s in (raw.get("signals_cited") or []) if s in present]
223:     weight = _clamp01(reliability * relevance)
224:     return CredibilityJudgment(
225:         evidence_id=str(row.get("evidence_id", "")),
226:         reliability_score=reliability,
227:         relevance_score=relevance,
228:         credibility_weight=weight if weight is not None else 0.0,
229:         rationale=str(raw.get("rationale", "")),
230:         signals_cited=cited,
231:         query_need=str(raw.get("query_need", "")),
232:         judge_error=False,
233:     )
234: 
235: 
236: def score_source_credibility(
237:     research_question: str,
238:     rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
239:     *,
240:     domain: str | None = None,
241:     judge: Optional[Callable[[str, dict], dict]] = None,
242: ) -> list[CredibilityJudgment]:
243:     """Score each source on reliability × relevance — ADVISORY ONLY, pure, no row mutation.
244: 
245:     With ``judge=None`` returns one priors-only judgment per row (no network, total + offline). With
246:     an injected ``judge(research_question, payload) -> dict`` runs it PER SOURCE so a judge error is
247:     isolated to one row. ``domain`` is forwarded only as the ``domain_hint`` payload field — there is
248:     no domain-keyed branch or rubric table (operator: no fixed rubrics).
249:     """
250:     if judge is None:
251:         return [_priors_only_judgment(row) for row in (rows or [])]
252:     return [_apply_judge(research_question, row, domain, judge) for row in (rows or [])]

 succeeded in 3832ms:
1: """I-cred-003 (Phase 3, L2 / §6c-2) — temporal / supersession + retraction hard penalty.
2: 
3: Pure-function credibility adjustment for time-sensitive evidence. It downgrades stale-but-authoritative
4: sources (old guidelines, superseded datasets/regulations) and applies a HARD penalty to
5: retracted/withdrawn sources, plus a per-claim ``soft_warning`` and a ``certainty_downgrade`` signal.
6: 
7: Default-OFF: this is inert library code — nothing in the production path imports or invokes it, so OFF
8: behaviour is byte-identical by construction. It acts only when a flagged caller (the Phase-2 scorer)
9: calls ``supersession_adjustment``; ``supersession_enabled()`` gates that wiring. It edits no
10: faithfulness-critical file (provenance_generator / strict_verify / 4-role / two-family / corpus_approval
11: are untouched). LAW VI: every threshold is an env-overridable named constant — no hardcoded magic
12: numbers, snake_case, explicit imports, no network, no live data.
13: """
14: 
15: from __future__ import annotations
16: 
17: import datetime
18: import os
19: from dataclasses import dataclass
20: 
21: # Runtime flag (default OFF). Both this and a caller invocation are needed for any effect.
22: _FLAG = "PG_SWEEP_SUPERSESSION"
23: 
24: # Source types whose credibility is TIME-SENSITIVE (a stale one is downgraded). Everything else
25: # (e.g. a foundational-theory paper) is NOT downgraded for age alone. Env-overridable (LAW VI).
26: _TIME_SENSITIVE_TYPES = frozenset(
27:     t.strip().lower()
28:     for t in os.getenv(
29:         "PG_SUPERSESSION_TIME_SENSITIVE_TYPES",
30:         "guideline,regulation,dataset,statistics,news,advisory",
31:     ).split(",")
32:     if t.strip()
33: )
34: 
35: 
36: def _env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
37:     raw = os.getenv(name)
38:     if raw is None or not raw.strip():
39:         return default
40:     try:
41:         return float(raw)
42:     except ValueError:
43:         return default
44: 
45: 
46: def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
47:     raw = os.getenv(name)
48:     if raw is None or not raw.strip():
49:         return default
50:     try:
51:         return int(raw)
52:     except ValueError:
53:         return default
54: 
55: 
56: def supersession_enabled() -> bool:
57:     """True iff the supersession layer is wired ON (default OFF => byte-identical)."""
58:     return os.getenv(_FLAG, "0") == "1"
59: 
60: 
61: @dataclass
62: class SupersessionResult:
63:     multiplier: float            # multiply the source's credibility weight by this (1.0 = no change)
64:     hard_penalty: bool           # True iff retracted/withdrawn (a near-zero multiplier)
65:     soft_warning: str | None     # surfaced per claim; None when no adjustment was made
66:     certainty_downgrade: bool    # True iff the claim's certainty label should drop
67: 
68: 
69: def _is_truthy(row: dict, *keys: str) -> bool:
70:     for key in keys:
71:         value = row.get(key)
72:         if isinstance(value, bool) and value:
73:             return True
74:         if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip().lower() in (
75:             "1", "true", "yes", "retracted", "withdrawn", "superseded",
76:         ):
77:             return True
78:     return False
79: 
80: 
81: def _publication_year(row: dict) -> int | None:
82:     for key in ("year", "publication_year", "pub_year"):
83:         value = row.get(key)
84:         if isinstance(value, int):
85:             return value
86:         if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip().isdigit():
87:             return int(value.strip())
88:     for key in ("date", "published", "publication_date"):
89:         value = row.get(key)
90:         if isinstance(value, str) and len(value) >= 4 and value[:4].isdigit():
91:             return int(value[:4])
92:     return None
93: 
94: 
95: def supersession_adjustment(row: dict, *, now_year: int | None = None) -> SupersessionResult:
96:     """Return the temporal/supersession credibility adjustment for one source row.
97: 
98:     ``row`` may carry a retraction flag (``is_retracted`` / ``retracted`` / ``retraction_notice`` /
99:     ``withdrawn``), a supersession flag (``is_superseded`` / ``superseded`` / ``superseded_by``), a
100:     source type (``source_type`` / ``type`` / ``tier_label``), and a publication year/date. ``now_year``
101:     defaults to the current UTC year — pass it explicitly for deterministic tests.
102:     """
103:     if now_year is None:
104:         now_year = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).year
105: 
106:     retraction_multiplier = _env_float("PG_SUPERSESSION_RETRACTION_MULTIPLIER", 0.05)
107:     stale_multiplier = _env_float("PG_SUPERSESSION_STALE_MULTIPLIER", 0.6)
108:     stale_years = _env_int("PG_SUPERSESSION_STALE_YEARS", 7)
109: 
110:     # 1) Retraction / withdrawal -> HARD penalty (overrides freshness + everything else).
111:     if _is_truthy(row, "is_retracted", "retracted", "retraction_notice", "withdrawn"):
112:         return SupersessionResult(
113:             multiplier=retraction_multiplier,
114:             hard_penalty=True,
115:             soft_warning="retracted/withdrawn source — hard credibility penalty applied",
116:             certainty_downgrade=True,
117:         )
118: 
119:     # 2) Explicitly superseded -> stale-grade downgrade.
120:     if _is_truthy(row, "is_superseded", "superseded") or row.get("superseded_by"):
121:         return SupersessionResult(
122:             multiplier=stale_multiplier,
123:             hard_penalty=False,
124:             soft_warning="superseded by a newer version — downgraded",
125:             certainty_downgrade=True,
126:         )
127: 
128:     # 3) Stale-by-age, ONLY for time-sensitive source types (no fabricated penalty otherwise).
129:     source_type = str(
130:         row.get("source_type") or row.get("type") or row.get("tier_label") or ""
131:     ).strip().lower()
132:     publication_year = _publication_year(row)
133:     if source_type in _TIME_SENSITIVE_TYPES and publication_year is not None:
134:         age = now_year - publication_year
135:         if age > stale_years:
136:             return SupersessionResult(
137:                 multiplier=stale_multiplier,
138:                 hard_penalty=False,
139:                 soft_warning=(
140:                     f"potentially out-of-date: {source_type} published {age} years ago "
141:                     f"(> {stale_years}y staleness threshold)"
142:                 ),
143:                 certainty_downgrade=True,
144:             )
145: 
146:     # 4) No adjustment.
147:     return SupersessionResult(multiplier=1.0, hard_penalty=False, soft_warning=None, certainty_downgrade=False)

 succeeded in 5645ms:
1: """Phase 4a (L3, §6c-4) — independence-collapse by content near-duplication.
2: 
3: Credibility-weighted sourcing redesign, plan
4: ``docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md`` Phase 4a.
5: 
6: WHAT THIS DOES
7: ==============
8: Given a list of generator-visible evidence rows, cluster the near-DUPLICATE
9: content copies (syndication / press-release rehash / verbatim republication)
10: into ``origin clusters`` so a later weighted tally (L5, Phase 6) counts each
11: independent ORIGIN exactly once and cannot be inflated by copies.
12: 
13: The collapse is layered on the existing host-collapse primitive
14: (``authority.corroboration``: ``count_independent_hosts`` /
15: ``registrable_domain``). Two rows join the same origin cluster when EITHER
16: 
17:   1. they share a registrable domain (eTLD+1 host-collapse — the existing
18:      primitive; subdomains of one institution are the same origin), OR
19:   2. their text content is a near-duplicate: TF-IDF cosine >= the configured
20:      similarity threshold (default ``DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD`` = 0.85),
21: 
22: EXCEPT a curated acceptable-mirror allowlist (arXiv / SSRN / PMC by default)
23: is NOT collapsed on content alone across DIFFERENT registrable domains — a
24: legitimate mirror of the same paper on two scholarly hosts stays two
25: independent origins rather than being falsely flagged a copy (the
26: echo-collapse false-positive bound, plan §6 RISKS).
27: 
28: CANONICAL-ORIGIN INVARIANT (the load-bearing safety property)
29: =============================================================
30: Each cluster designates exactly ONE **canonical origin**. When ANY member
31: carries a parseable publication date, the canonical is the EARLIEST-dated
32: member and ``authority_score`` is NOT consulted — a DATED cluster keeps
33: STRICT copy-invariance (adding a same/later/undated copy of ANY authority
34: leaves the canonical + cluster_mass unchanged). When EVERY member is undated
35: there is no date to identify the seed, so the canonical is the LOWEST-
36: ``authority_score`` member (conservative-min, Codex #1161): a higher-authority
37: copy can NEVER become canonical or inflate cluster_mass, and the worst a copy
38: can do is LOWER the mass (monotonic non-increase) — never inflate.
39: 
40: Therefore: adding a copied row to an existing cluster — **even a copy whose
41: own ``authority_score`` is HIGHER than the cluster's canonical origin** —
42: does NOT change the cluster set nor its canonical origin nor inflate its
43: mass. A high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own
44: *independent* content
45: would form a new cluster. This is exactly what lets the L5 weighted tally
46: (``cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical_origin)``, copies contribute
47: zero) be uninflatable by copies. The invariant is proven by
48: ``test_independence_collapse.py``.
49: 
50: OUTPUT (per the Phase-4a emitted contract)
51: ==========================================
52: ``collapse_independent_origins`` returns an :class:`IndependenceCollapseResult`
53: carrying, for every input row (by index):
54:   - a stable ``origin_cluster_id`` (deterministic, derived from the canonical
55:     member so it is stable under copy-additions),
56:   - the canonical-origin row index for that cluster,
57:   - an ``is_canonical_origin`` / ``is_derivative_copy`` flag,
58: and an ``independent_origin_count`` = number of distinct origin clusters.
59: 
60: PURITY / SAFETY
61: ===============
62: Pure: constructs no client, opens no network, loads no model, calls no LLM,
63: mutates none of the caller's rows (membership/flags are emitted in a fresh
64: structure). snake_case, explicit imports, no host/TLD literals in code (the
65: PSL ``gov_suffixes`` and the mirror allowlist are passed in by the caller).
66: 
67: DEFAULT-OFF: this module is inert library code. Nothing in the production
68: pipeline imports or invokes it; it acts ONLY when a flagged caller calls
69: ``collapse_independent_origins``. It touches NO faithfulness gate
70: (strict_verify / 4-role / two-family / corpus_approval are untouched) and
71: NEVER drops a source — it only annotates duplicated corroboration.
72: """
73: from __future__ import annotations
74: 
75: import datetime
76: import math
77: import re
78: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
79: from typing import Any
80: from urllib.parse import urlparse
81: 
82: from src.polaris_graph.authority.corroboration import registrable_domain
83: 
84: # ── Module constants (LAW VI — named, not magic numbers) ────────────────────
85: # Cosine-similarity threshold at/above which two texts are a content copy.
86: DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD: float = 0.85
87: # Curated acceptable-mirror domains: legitimate cross-host mirrors of the same
88: # scholarly artefact that must NOT be over-collapsed on content alone (plan §6
89: # echo-collapse false-positive bound). Passed-in overrides win; this is only
90: # the documented default.
91: #
92: # IMPORTANT: these are compared against each row's REGISTRABLE DOMAIN (the
93: # eTLD+1 produced by ``corroboration.registrable_domain``), so they MUST be
94: # written in eTLD+1 form. PMC lives at ``ncbi.nlm.nih.gov``, whose eTLD+1
95: # (without a multi-level PSL suffix match) collapses to ``nih.gov`` — so the
96: # allowlist entry for PMC is ``nih.gov`` (the registrable-domain key actually
97: # seen at comparison time), NOT the full ``ncbi.nlm.nih.gov`` host. Writing the
98: # full host here would silently never match and re-open the false-positive.
99: DEFAULT_ACCEPTABLE_MIRROR_DOMAINS: tuple[str, ...] = (
100:     "arxiv.org",   # arXiv preprint mirror
101:     "ssrn.com",    # SSRN working-paper mirror
102:     "nih.gov",     # PMC (ncbi.nlm.nih.gov) eTLD+1 -> nih.gov
103: )
104: # Row keys consulted for the row's text, in priority order. First non-empty
105: # wins. Mirrors finding_dedup's row-shape expectations.
106: _TEXT_KEYS: tuple[str, ...] = ("direct_quote", "statement", "text", "snippet")
107: # Row keys consulted for an explicit publication-order signal, in priority
108: # order. First parseable value wins; absence falls back to corpus order.
109: _ORDER_KEYS: tuple[str, ...] = (
110:     "published_date",
111:     "publication_date",
112:     "published",
113:     "date",
114: )
115: # A token is a maximal run of word characters; lowercased. Pure-stdlib
116: # tokeniser — no model, no network.
117: _TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W_]+", re.UNICODE)

155: 
156: def _host_of(url: str) -> str:
157:     """Bare hostname (lowercased, ``www.`` stripped) from a URL; ``""`` if none.
158: 
159:     ``registrable_domain`` expects a HOST, not a full URL, so this reduction
160:     must run first (mirrors ``finding_dedup._host_of``).
161:     """
162:     if not url:
163:         return ""
164:     host = (urlparse(url).hostname or "").lower().strip()
165:     if host.startswith("www."):
166:         host = host[4:]
167:     return host
168: 
169: 
170: def _row_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
171:     """First non-empty text field of a row (priority order ``_TEXT_KEYS``)."""
172:     for key in _TEXT_KEYS:
173:         value = row.get(key)
174:         if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip():
175:             return value
176:     return ""
177: 
178: 
179: def _row_domain(row: dict[str, Any], gov_suffixes: tuple[str, ...]) -> str:
180:     """Registrable domain (eTLD+1) for a row's ``source_url``; ``""`` if none."""
181:     return registrable_domain(_host_of(str(row.get("source_url", ""))), gov_suffixes)
182: 
183: 
184: def _parse_order_date(value: Any) -> tuple[int, int, int] | None:
185:     """Parse a publication-order value to a sortable ``(year, month, day)``, or ``None``
186:     when it is not CONFIDENTLY parseable (then the row is treated as undated and sorts
187:     last). Only UNAMBIGUOUS year-first formats are accepted — ISO ``YYYY-MM-DD`` /
188:     ``YYYY/MM/DD`` / ``YYYY-MM`` / ``YYYY``, or an int/float year. Ambiguous or malformed
189:     values (e.g. ``01/01/2099`` day-first, or free text) are REJECTED so they cannot
190:     lexically outrank a real ISO date and steal the canonical origin (Codex iter-2 P1).
191:     """
192:     if isinstance(value, bool):
193:         return None
194:     if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
195:         year = int(value)
196:         return (year, 1, 1) if 1000 <= year <= 9999 else None
197:     if not isinstance(value, str):
198:         return None
199:     match = re.match(r"^(\d{4})(?:[-/](\d{1,2})(?:[-/](\d{1,2}))?)?$", value.strip())
200:     if not match:
201:         return None
202:     year = int(match.group(1))
203:     month = int(match.group(2) or 1)
204:     day = int(match.group(3) or 1)
205:     try:
206:         # REAL calendar validation — an ISO-shaped but non-calendar date (e.g. 2024-02-31,
207:         # Feb has no 31st; or 2024-13-01) raises and is treated as undated, so it cannot
208:         # outrank a genuine date and steal the canonical origin (Codex iter-3 P1).
209:         datetime.date(year, month, day)
210:     except ValueError:
211:         return None
212:     return (year, month, day)
213: 
214: 
215: def _order_key(row: dict[str, Any], row_index: int) -> tuple:
216:     """Deterministic canonical-selection order key (canonical = ``min`` over members).
217: 
218:     A DATED row sorts before any undated row; among dated rows the earliest CALENDAR date
219:     wins and ``authority_score`` is NEVER consulted (so a higher-authority but later-dated
220:     copy can never steal the canonical origin). Same-date ties break on the STABLE
221:     evidence_id, then the input index. Codex iter-1/2/3 fixes: dated-beats-undated;
222:     malformed/non-ISO and non-calendar dates are treated as undated.
223: 
224:     When EVERY member of a cluster is undated there is no date to identify the seed
225:     positionally, so the canonical is the LOWEST-``authority_score`` member: a higher-
226:     authority copy can then never become the canonical origin and inflate ``cluster_mass``
227:     (the load-bearing copy-invariance invariant — Codex iter-4 P1). The evidence_id breaks
228:     authority ties (NOT the input index), so a prepended copy cannot win on position. A
229:     later-added LOWER-authority copy may relabel the cluster to itself, but that only ever
230:     LOWERS the mass — monotonically non-increasing under additions, never inflatable.
231: 
232:     Returns a 5-tuple ``(has_no_date, (y, m, d), auth_rank, evidence_id, row_index)``; the
233:     ``auth_rank`` slot is a constant ``0.0`` for DATED rows (date decides) and the real
234:     authority for undated rows (lowest wins).
235:     """
236:     eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
237:     for key in _ORDER_KEYS:
238:         parsed = _parse_order_date(row.get(key))
239:         if parsed is not None:
240:             return (0, parsed, 0.0, eid, row_index)
241:     authority = row.get("authority_score")
242:     auth_rank = float(authority) if isinstance(authority, (int, float)) else 0.0
243:     return (1, (9999, 12, 31), auth_rank, eid, row_index)
244: 
245: 
246: def _tokenize(text: str) -> list[str]:
247:     """Lowercased word-character tokens. Pure stdlib; deterministic."""
248:     return [t.lower() for t in _TOKEN_RE.findall(text)]
249: 
250: 
251: def _term_frequencies(tokens: list[str]) -> dict[str, int]:
252:     counts: dict[str, int] = {}
253:     for tok in tokens:
254:         counts[tok] = counts.get(tok, 0) + 1
255:     return counts
256: 
257: 
258: def _tfidf_cosine_matrix(texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
259:     """Pairwise TF-IDF cosine similarity over ``texts`` (pure Python).
260: 
261:     TF = raw term count per document. IDF = ``ln((1 + N) / (1 + df)) + 1``
262:     (the smoothed, always-positive scikit-learn convention) so a single-doc
263:     corpus still yields well-defined, non-degenerate vectors. Vectors are
264:     L2-normalised; cosine of two normalised vectors is their dot product.
265:     An empty document has a zero vector and cosine 0 with everything.
266:     """
267:     n = len(texts)
268:     token_lists = [_tokenize(t) for t in texts]
269:     # Document frequency per term.
270:     doc_freq: dict[str, int] = {}
271:     for tokens in token_lists:
272:         for term in set(tokens):
273:             doc_freq[term] = doc_freq.get(term, 0) + 1
274:     idf: dict[str, float] = {
275:         term: math.log((1 + n) / (1 + df)) + 1.0 for term, df in doc_freq.items()
276:     }
277:     # Build L2-normalised TF-IDF vectors as sparse dicts.
278:     vectors: list[dict[str, float]] = []
279:     for tokens in token_lists:
280:         tf = _term_frequencies(tokens)
281:         vec = {term: count * idf[term] for term, count in tf.items()}
282:         norm = math.sqrt(sum(w * w for w in vec.values()))
283:         if norm > 0.0:
284:             vec = {term: w / norm for term, w in vec.items()}
285:         vectors.append(vec)
286:     # Pairwise cosine = dot product of normalised vectors.
287:     matrix = [[0.0] * n for _ in range(n)]
288:     for i in range(n):
289:         matrix[i][i] = 1.0 if vectors[i] else 0.0
290:         for j in range(i + 1, n):
291:             a, b = vectors[i], vectors[j]
292:             # Iterate the smaller dict for the dot product.
293:             if len(a) > len(b):
294:                 a, b = b, a
295:             dot = sum(weight * b.get(term, 0.0) for term, weight in a.items())
296:             sim = 0.0 if dot < 0.0 else (1.0 if dot > 1.0 else dot)
297:             matrix[i][j] = sim
298:             matrix[j][i] = sim
299:     return matrix
300: 
301: 
302: class _UnionFind:
303:     """Minimal disjoint-set for transitive content-copy clustering."""
304: 
305:     def __init__(self, n: int) -> None:
306:         self._parent = list(range(n))
307: 
308:     def find(self, x: int) -> int:
309:         root = x
310:         while self._parent[root] != root:
311:             root = self._parent[root]
312:         # Path compression.
313:         while self._parent[x] != root:
314:             self._parent[x], x = root, self._parent[x]
315:         return root
316: 
317:     def union(self, a: int, b: int) -> None:
318:         ra, rb = self.find(a), self.find(b)
319:         if ra != rb:
320:             # Attach the higher-index root under the lower so the canonical
321:             # (lowest order-key) tends to stay near the root; canonical choice
322:             # below is explicit regardless, this only keeps roots stable.
323:             lo, hi = (ra, rb) if ra < rb else (rb, ra)
324:             self._parent[hi] = lo
325: 
326: 
327: # ── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
328: 
329: 
330: def collapse_independent_origins(
331:     rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
332:     *,
333:     gov_suffixes: tuple[str, ...],
334:     similarity_threshold: float = DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD,
335:     acceptable_mirror_domains: tuple[str, ...] = DEFAULT_ACCEPTABLE_MIRROR_DOMAINS,
336: ) -> IndependenceCollapseResult:
337:     """Cluster rows into independent origins; flag derivative copies.
338: 
339:     Args:
340:         rows: generator-visible evidence rows. Each may carry ``evidence_id``,
341:             ``source_url``, text (``direct_quote``/``statement``/``text``/
342:             ``snippet``), an optional publication-order key
343:             (``published_date``/``publication_date``/``published``/``date``),
344:             and an optional ``authority_score`` (NOT used for clustering or
345:             canonical choice — only carried through by the caller).
346:         gov_suffixes: PSL multi-level gov-suffix tuple from
347:             ``authority.data_loader.load_authority_data()["psl_gov_suffixes"]``
348:             — passed in so this module hardcodes NO host/TLD literals.
349:         similarity_threshold: TF-IDF cosine at/above which two texts are a
350:             content copy (LAW VI — caller-configurable; default 0.85).
351:         acceptable_mirror_domains: registrable domains whose cross-host
352:             content near-duplicates are legitimate mirrors and are NOT
353:             collapsed on content alone (false-positive bound).
354: 
355:     Returns:
356:         :class:`IndependenceCollapseResult` with one
357:         :class:`RowOriginAssignment` per input row (ordered by row index),
358:         the :class:`OriginCluster` list, and the ``independent_origin_count``.
359:     """
360:     rows = list(rows or [])
361:     n = len(rows)
362:     threshold = float(similarity_threshold)
363:     mirror_set = {d.lower() for d in acceptable_mirror_domains}
364: 
365:     if n == 0:
366:         return IndependenceCollapseResult(
367:             clusters=[],
368:             assignments=[],
369:             raw_row_count=0,
370:             independent_origin_count=0,
371:             similarity_threshold=threshold,
372:         )
373: 
374:     domains = [_row_domain(rows[i], gov_suffixes) for i in range(n)]
375:     texts = [_row_text(rows[i]) for i in range(n)]
376:     sim = _tfidf_cosine_matrix(texts)
377: 
378:     uf = _UnionFind(n)
379:     for i in range(n):
380:         for j in range(i + 1, n):
381:             same_domain = bool(domains[i]) and domains[i] == domains[j]
382:             if same_domain:
383:                 # Same registrable origin (host-collapse primitive): one origin.
384:                 uf.union(i, j)
385:                 continue
386:             # Cross-domain: collapse only on content near-duplication. A curated
387:             # acceptable-mirror host (arXiv/SSRN/PMC) only ever collapses with its
388:             # OWN registrable domain (the same_domain branch above), NEVER via
389:             # cross-domain content. Otherwise a non-mirror blog that copies the
390:             # content of TWO different mirror hosts would TRANSITIVELY bridge them
391:             # into one union-find cluster (Codex iter-1 P2). Skipping any
392:             # cross-domain union that touches a mirror keeps legitimate mirrors
393:             # independent on content alone; the rare non-mirror copy of mirror
394:             # content simply stays its own origin — a safe under-collapse, never a
395:             # mirror false-merge.
396:             either_mirror = domains[i] in mirror_set or domains[j] in mirror_set
397:             if either_mirror:
398:                 continue
399:             if texts[i] and texts[j] and sim[i][j] >= threshold:
400:                 uf.union(i, j)
401: 
402:     # Group members by union-find root.
403:     members_by_root: dict[int, list[int]] = {}
404:     for i in range(n):
405:         members_by_root.setdefault(uf.find(i), []).append(i)
406: 
407:     clusters: list[OriginCluster] = []
408:     canonical_of: dict[int, int] = {}      # member index -> canonical index
409:     cluster_id_of: dict[int, str] = {}     # member index -> origin_cluster_id
410: 
411:     for member_indices in members_by_root.values():
412:         member_indices = sorted(member_indices)
413:         # Canonical = earliest-dated origin (authority not consulted), or for an all-undated
414:         # cluster the LOWEST-authority member (conservative-min, no inflation — Codex #1161).
415:         canonical_index = min(
416:             member_indices, key=lambda idx: _order_key(rows[idx], idx)
417:         )
418:         # Stable, copy-immune id: derived from the canonical row's EVIDENCE IDENTITY, NOT its
419:         # input position — so a copy added BEFORE the canonical (prepended) does not shift the
420:         # index and change the id (Codex iter-2 P2). A missing evidence_id is a FAIL-LOUD data
421:         # error (Codex #1161), never a position-relative fallback.
422:         canonical_eid = str(rows[canonical_index].get("evidence_id", "") or "").strip()
423:         if not canonical_eid:
424:             raise ValueError(
425:                 "independence_collapse: canonical row is missing 'evidence_id'; a stable "
426:                 "origin_cluster_id requires every evidence row to carry evidence_id "
427:                 "(Codex #1161 — a positional fallback id is not copy-stable)."
428:             )
429:         origin_cluster_id = f"origin::{canonical_eid}"
430:         copy_indices = [i for i in member_indices if i != canonical_index]
431:         member_hosts = sorted({domains[i] for i in member_indices} - {""})
432:         clusters.append(
433:             OriginCluster(
434:                 origin_cluster_id=origin_cluster_id,
435:                 canonical_index=canonical_index,
436:                 member_indices=member_indices,
437:                 copy_indices=copy_indices,
438:                 member_hosts=member_hosts,
439:             )
440:         )
441:         for i in member_indices:
442:             canonical_of[i] = canonical_index
443:             cluster_id_of[i] = origin_cluster_id
444: 
445:     clusters.sort(key=lambda c: c.canonical_index)
446: 
447:     assignments = [
448:         RowOriginAssignment(
449:             row_index=i,
450:             origin_cluster_id=cluster_id_of[i],
451:             canonical_index=canonical_of[i],
452:             is_canonical_origin=(canonical_of[i] == i),
453:             is_derivative_copy=(canonical_of[i] != i),
454:         )
455:         for i in range(n)
456:     ]
457: 
458:     return IndependenceCollapseResult(
459:         clusters=clusters,
460:         assignments=assignments,
461:         raw_row_count=n,
462:         independent_origin_count=len(clusters),
463:         similarity_threshold=threshold,
464:     )
