 succeeded in 2547ms:
250:     numeric_extractor: Callable[..., list[ExtractedNumericClaim]] = extract_numeric_claims,
251:     qualitative_extractor: Callable[..., list[QualitativeAssertion]] = extract_qualitative_assertions,
252: ) -> list[AtomicClaim]:
253:     """Field-agnostic atomic-claim extraction over evidence ``rows``.
254: 
255:     Composes the injected numeric + qualitative extractors and ALWAYS falls back to a
256:     conservative raw-text claim, so EVERY non-empty row yields >=1 atomic claim and
257:     nothing is silently dropped. The extractors are dependency-injected (real
258:     defaults; tests pass fakes / fixtures) — keeping this function pure + offline.
259: 
260:     Args:
261:         rows: evidence rows (each a dict with at least ``evidence_id`` and a text
262:             field — ``direct_quote`` / ``statement`` / ``text``).
263:         domain: optional domain hint forwarded to the numeric extractor (it routes to
264:             a broader predicate set for non-clinical queries).
265:         numeric_extractor: ``(rows, domain) -> list[ExtractedNumericClaim]``.
266:         qualitative_extractor: ``(rows, domain) -> list[QualitativeAssertion]``.
267: 
268:     Returns:
269:         ``list[AtomicClaim]`` with ``claim_cluster_id`` still "" (assigned by
270:         ``cluster_equivalent_claims``). A row that yields a structured claim does NOT
271:         additionally yield a raw claim (avoid double-counting); a row that yields NO
272:         structured claim yields exactly one raw singleton claim.
273:     """
274:     rows = list(rows or [])
275:     out: list[AtomicClaim] = []
276:     structured_evidence_ids: set[str] = set()
277: 
278:     # 1. numeric extractor (per-row so we can attribute claim ids deterministically).
279:     for row in rows:
280:         evid = str(row.get("evidence_id", ""))
281:         try:
282:             numeric_claims = numeric_extractor([row], domain) if domain is not None \
283:                 else numeric_extractor([row])
284:         except TypeError:
285:             # an injected extractor that does not accept a domain kwarg
286:             numeric_claims = numeric_extractor([row])
287:         for ci, nc in enumerate(numeric_claims):
288:             structured_evidence_ids.add(evid)
289:             out.append(AtomicClaim(
290:                 evidence_id=evid,
291:                 kind="numeric",
292:                 subject=(getattr(nc, "subject", "") or "").strip().lower(),
293:                 predicate=(getattr(nc, "predicate", "") or "").strip().lower(),
294:                 normalized_key=_normalized_key_numeric(nc, evid, ci),
295:                 text=str(getattr(nc, "context_snippet", "") or _row_text(row))[:200],
296:                 source_url=str(getattr(nc, "source_url", "") or row.get("source_url", "")),
297:                 source_tier=str(getattr(nc, "source_tier", "") or row.get("tier", "")),
298:             ))
299: 
300:     # 2. qualitative extractor (whole-corpus; one row may yield several assertions).
301:     try:
302:         qual_assertions = qualitative_extractor(rows, domain) if domain is not None \
303:             else qualitative_extractor(rows)
304:     except TypeError:
305:         qual_assertions = qualitative_extractor(rows)
306:     # group qualitative assertions by evidence_id so the per-claim sentinel index is
307:     # stable + distinct per row.
308:     qual_index_by_evid: dict[str, int] = {}
309:     for qa in qual_assertions:
310:         evid = str(getattr(qa, "evidence_id", "") or "")
311:         ci = qual_index_by_evid.get(evid, 0)
312:         qual_index_by_evid[evid] = ci + 1
313:         structured_evidence_ids.add(evid)
314:         out.append(AtomicClaim(
315:             evidence_id=evid,
316:             kind="qualitative",
317:             subject=(getattr(qa, "subject", "") or "").strip().lower(),
318:             predicate=(getattr(qa, "concept_type", "") or "").strip().lower(),
319:             normalized_key=_normalized_key_qualitative(qa, evid, ci),
320:             text=str(getattr(qa, "context_snippet", "") or "")[:200],
321:             source_url=str(getattr(qa, "source_url", "") or ""),
322:             source_tier=str(getattr(qa, "source_tier", "") or ""),
323:         ))
324: 
325:     # 3. conservative raw fallback: any row that produced NO structured claim yields
326:     #    exactly one raw singleton, keyed by (evidence_id, normalized text), so it is
327:     #    NEVER merged and NEVER dropped. Field-agnostic coverage guarantee.
328:     for row in rows:
329:         evid = str(row.get("evidence_id", ""))
330:         text = _row_text(row)
331:         if not text.strip():
332:             continue
333:         if evid in structured_evidence_ids:
334:             continue
335:         out.append(AtomicClaim(
336:             evidence_id=evid,
337:             kind="raw",
338:             subject="",
339:             predicate="",
340:             normalized_key=("__raw__", evid, _norm_text_key(text)),
341:             text=text[:200],
342:             source_url=str(row.get("source_url", "")),
343:             source_tier=str(row.get("tier", "")),
344:         ))
345: 
346:     return out
347: 
348: 
349: def _claim_cluster_id(normalized_key: tuple) -> str:
350:     """Deterministic, stable id for an equivalence cluster.
351: 
352:     SHA-1 over a canonical string rendering of the normalized key, truncated +
353:     prefixed. Deterministic (no uuid/random/time), so the SAME key always yields the
354:     SAME id across runs — required for the downstream P6 join on
355:     ``(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)``.
356:     """
357:     canonical = repr(tuple(normalized_key))
358:     digest = hashlib.sha1(canonical.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:_CLAIM_ID_HASH_LEN]
359:     return f"{_CLAIM_ID_PREFIX}{digest}"
360: 
361: 
362: def cluster_equivalent_claims(
363:     claims: list[AtomicClaim],
364: ) -> dict[str, list[int]]:
365:     """Group EQUIVALENT atomic claims under one stable ``claim_cluster_id``.
366: 
367:     Conservative-singleton safety: claims share a cluster ONLY when their
368:     ``normalized_key`` is EQUAL. Per-claim sentinel keys (unknown subject / raw text)
369:     are unique by construction, so such claims are always singletons — two distinct
370:     claims NEVER over-merge. MUTATES each claim's ``claim_cluster_id`` in place (the
371:     claims are this module's own objects, never the caller's evidence rows).
372: 
373:     Returns ``{claim_cluster_id: [member indices into ``claims``]}``.
374:     """
375:     clusters: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
376:     for idx, claim in enumerate(claims):
377:         cid = _claim_cluster_id(claim.normalized_key)
378:         claim.claim_cluster_id = cid
379:         clusters.setdefault(cid, []).append(idx)
380:     return clusters
381: 
382: 
383: # ── contradiction / refutation edges (recall-first) ───────────────────────────
384: 
385: 
386: def _cluster_id_for_evidence(claims: list[AtomicClaim]) -> dict[str, set[str]]:
387:     """Map evidence_id -> the set of claim_cluster_ids its claims belong to (fallback only)."""
388:     out: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
389:     for claim in claims:
390:         out.setdefault(claim.evidence_id, set()).add(claim.claim_cluster_id)
391:     return out
392: 
393: 
394: def _cluster_ids_by_subject(claims: list[AtomicClaim]) -> dict[tuple, set[str]]:
395:     """Map ``(evidence_id, subject)`` -> claim_cluster_ids.
396: 
397:     Used to attach a contradiction edge ONLY to the clusters that share the edge's
398:     SUBJECT on an endpoint row — so when a row hosts several distinct atomic claims, a
399:     contradiction about one subject does NOT pull in the unrelated clusters of a
400:     DIFFERENT subject on the same row (Codex iter-1 P2). Subject is normalized
401:     (strip+lower) to match both the edge record's subject and the AtomicClaim subject.
402:     """
403:     out: dict[tuple, set[str]] = {}
404:     for claim in claims:
405:         sig = (claim.evidence_id, (claim.subject or "").strip().lower())
406:         out.setdefault(sig, set()).add(claim.claim_cluster_id)
407:     return out
408: 
409: 
410: def _edge_cluster_pair(
411:     subject: str,
412:     evidence_ids: tuple,
413:     cluster_by_subject: dict[tuple, set[str]],
414:     cluster_by_evid: dict[str, set[str]],
415: ) -> tuple:
416:     """Sorted claim_cluster_ids of the SUBJECT-matching claims on the endpoint rows.
417: 
418:     For each endpoint evidence row, attach the clusters whose claims share the edge's
419:     subject. If an endpoint has no subject-matching claim (e.g. an empty-subject
420:     semantic edge), fall back to that row's clusters so the edge is never lost
421:     (recall-first) — coarser only in that rare case, never the common multi-subject-row
422:     contamination (Codex iter-1 P2).
423:     """
424:     subj = (subject or "").strip().lower()
425:     ids: set[str] = set()
426:     for evid in evidence_ids:
427:         matched = cluster_by_subject.get((str(evid), subj), set())
428:         ids.update(matched if matched else cluster_by_evid.get(str(evid), set()))
429:     return tuple(sorted(ids))
430: 
431: 
432: def build_contradiction_edges(
433:     rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
434:     claims: list[AtomicClaim],
435:     *,
436:     domain: str | None = None,
437:     nli_judge: Optional[Callable[[str, str], tuple]] = None,
438:     numeric_extractor: Callable[..., list[ExtractedNumericClaim]] = extract_numeric_claims,
439:     qualitative_extractor: Callable[..., list[QualitativeAssertion]] = extract_qualitative_assertions,
440: ) -> list[ContradictionEdge]:
441:     """Build contradiction / refutation edges over the atomic claims — RECALL-FIRST.
442: 
443:     Reuses the three existing detectors as edge sources (used, not re-implemented):
444:       * numeric: ``detect_contradictions`` over ``extract_numeric_claims`` — a
445:         >threshold numeric disagreement on the same (subject, predicate, unit, dose).
446:       * qualitative: ``detect_qualitative_conflicts`` over
447:         ``extract_qualitative_assertions`` — present-vs-absent assertion-status
448:         conflicts + review flags (the no-number lethal-miss class).
449:       * semantic (OPTIONAL): only when an ``nli_judge`` is injected. Clusters rows by
450:         shared salient words, judges pairs, keeps ``contradict`` pairs. The judge is
451:         ``(claim_a, claim_b) -> (label, confidence)``; its per-pair fail-open (a judge
452:         ERROR skips THAT pair) is the only place an edge can be missed, and it is
453:         confined to the LLM path — the deterministic numeric + qualitative edges are
454:         never suppressed. NO judge is constructed here: off-mode / no-judge ⇒ no
455:         network, no spend (the production judge is wired by the caller, not this
456:         pure library).
457: 
458:     Recall-first: we OVER-detect (coarse review flags included) and NEVER silently
459:     drop a real conflict. Endpoints are attached to their ``claim_cluster_id`` via the
460:     already-clustered ``claims`` so a downstream vote (P6) sees the conflict.
461: 
462:     Returns ``list[ContradictionEdge]``, de-duplicated by
463:     (source, subject, predicate, sorted endpoint evidence_ids).
464:     """
465:     rows = list(rows or [])
466:     cluster_by_evid = _cluster_id_for_evidence(claims)
467:     cluster_by_subject = _cluster_ids_by_subject(claims)
468:     edges: list[ContradictionEdge] = []
469:     seen: set[tuple] = set()
470: 
471:     def _add(source: str, subject: str, predicate: str,
472:              evidence_ids: list, severity: str) -> None:
473:         evid_tuple = tuple(sorted(str(e) for e in evidence_ids if str(e)))
474:         if len(evid_tuple) < 2:
475:             return  # an edge needs two distinct endpoints
476:         dedup_key = (source, subject, predicate, evid_tuple)
477:         if dedup_key in seen:
478:             return
479:         seen.add(dedup_key)
480:         edges.append(ContradictionEdge(
481:             source=source,
482:             subject=subject,
483:             predicate=predicate,
484:             evidence_ids=evid_tuple,
485:             claim_cluster_ids=_edge_cluster_pair(
486:                 subject, evid_tuple, cluster_by_subject, cluster_by_evid
487:             ),
488:             severity=str(severity or "review"),
489:         ))
490: 
491:     # 1. numeric contradictions (deterministic, recall-first within its threshold).
492:     try:
493:         numeric_claims = numeric_extractor(rows, domain) if domain is not None \
494:             else numeric_extractor(rows)
495:     except TypeError:
496:         numeric_claims = numeric_extractor(rows)
497:     for rec in detect_contradictions(numeric_claims):
498:         _add(
499:             "numeric",
500:             getattr(rec, "subject", ""),
501:             getattr(rec, "predicate", ""),
502:             [getattr(c, "evidence_id", "") for c in getattr(rec, "claims", [])],
503:             getattr(rec, "severity", "review"),
504:         )
505: 
506:     # 2. qualitative conflicts (present-vs-absent + review flags, never silent-drop).
507:     try:
508:         qual_assertions = qualitative_extractor(rows, domain) if domain is not None \
509:             else qualitative_extractor(rows)
510:     except TypeError:
511:         qual_assertions = qualitative_extractor(rows)
512:     for rec in detect_qualitative_conflicts(qual_assertions):
513:         _add(
514:             "qualitative",
515:             getattr(rec, "subject", ""),
516:             getattr(rec, "predicate", ""),
517:             [c.get("evidence_id", "") for c in getattr(rec, "claims", [])],
518:             getattr(rec, "severity", "review"),
519:         )
520: 
521:     # 3. semantic NLI conflicts — ONLY when a judge is injected (no judge ⇒ no spend).
522:     if nli_judge is not None:
523:         clusters = cluster_candidate_rows(
524:             rows,
525:             min_overlap=_int_env(_ENV_NLI_MIN_OVERLAP, _DEFAULT_NLI_MIN_OVERLAP),
526:             max_rows=_int_env(_ENV_NLI_MAX_ROWS, _DEFAULT_NLI_MAX_ROWS),
527:         )
528:         if clusters:
529:             pairs = extract_pairs(
530:                 clusters,
531:                 max_pairs=_int_env(_ENV_NLI_MAX_PAIRS, _DEFAULT_NLI_MAX_PAIRS),
532:             )
533:             sem_records = detect_semantic_conflicts(
534:                 pairs,
535:                 nli_judge,
536:                 min_confidence=_float_env(
537:                     _ENV_NLI_MIN_CONFIDENCE, _DEFAULT_NLI_MIN_CONFIDENCE
538:                 ),
539:             )
540:             for rec in sem_records:
541:                 _add(
542:                     "semantic",
543:                     getattr(rec, "subject", ""),
544:                     getattr(rec, "predicate", ""),
545:                     [c.get("evidence_id", "") for c in getattr(rec, "claims", [])],
546:                     getattr(rec, "severity", "review"),
547:                 )
548: 
549:     return edges
550: 
551: 
552: def build_claim_graph(
553:     rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
554:     *,
555:     domain: str | None = None,
556:     nli_judge: Optional[Callable[[str, str], tuple]] = None,
557:     numeric_extractor: Callable[..., list[ExtractedNumericClaim]] = extract_numeric_claims,
558:     qualitative_extractor: Callable[..., list[QualitativeAssertion]] = extract_qualitative_assertions,
559: ) -> ClaimGraph:
560:     """End-to-end: extract atomic claims -> cluster equivalents -> build edges.
561: 
562:     Pure orchestration over the building blocks above. The edge-source detectors +
563:     the optional NLI judge are dependency-injected so the whole graph builds offline
564:     with fakes / fixtures. No flag check here — the caller gates the invocation via
565:     ``claim_graph_enabled()`` (default-OFF), keeping this a pure library function.
566:     """
567:     claims = extract_atomic_claims(
568:         rows,
569:         domain=domain,
570:         numeric_extractor=numeric_extractor,
571:         qualitative_extractor=qualitative_extractor,
572:     )
573:     clusters = cluster_equivalent_claims(claims)
574:     edges = build_contradiction_edges(
575:         rows,
576:         claims,
577:         domain=domain,
578:         nli_judge=nli_judge,
579:         numeric_extractor=numeric_extractor,
580:         qualitative_extractor=qualitative_extractor,
581:     )
582:     return ClaimGraph(
583:         claims=claims,
584:         clusters=clusters,
585:         edges=edges,
586:         raw_row_count=len(list(rows or [])),
587:         distinct_cluster_count=len(clusters),
588:     )

129: @dataclass
130: class AtomicClaim:
131:     """One field-agnostic atomic claim extracted + normalized from one evidence row.
132: 
133:     ``claim_cluster_id`` groups EQUIVALENT claims (assigned by
134:     ``cluster_equivalent_claims``); it is "" until clustering runs. ``normalized_key``
135:     is the conservative grouping key (the per-claim sentinel for an ``unknown``
136:     subject / a raw-text claim — never mergeable). ``kind`` records which extractor
137:     produced the claim (``numeric`` / ``qualitative`` / ``raw``) for audit.
138:     """
139: 
140:     evidence_id: str
141:     kind: str                      # "numeric" | "qualitative" | "raw"
142:     subject: str
143:     predicate: str
144:     normalized_key: tuple          # conservative grouping key (see _normalized_key_*)
145:     text: str                      # original snippet / quote for display
146:     source_url: str = ""
147:     source_tier: str = ""
148:     claim_cluster_id: str = ""     # set by cluster_equivalent_claims
149: 
150: 
151: @dataclass
152: class ContradictionEdge:
153:     """A contradiction / refutation edge between two atomic claims (recall-first).
154: 
155:     ``source`` is one of ``numeric`` / ``qualitative`` / ``semantic`` — which detector
156:     produced the edge. ``claim_cluster_ids`` is the (sorted) pair of cluster ids the
157:     two sides belong to. ``severity`` is the producing detector's own severity label.
158:     The two endpoints are kept as evidence ids so an edge survives serialization.
159:     """
160: 
161:     source: str
162:     subject: str
163:     predicate: str
164:     evidence_ids: tuple            # the two (or more) endpoint evidence_ids
165:     claim_cluster_ids: tuple       # sorted pair of claim_cluster_id endpoints
166:     severity: str = "review"
167: 
168:

170: class ClaimGraph:
171:     """The full claim-graph: atomic claims + equivalence clusters + contradiction edges."""

174:     # claim_cluster_id -> the indices (into ``claims``) of its member claims
175:     clusters: dict[str, list[int]]

1: """I-cred-006 (Phase 6, L5) — origin-cluster weight-mass aggregator (pure module).
2: 
3: Aggregate, per claim cluster, a **weight-mass** = Σ over INDEPENDENT origin clusters of
4: ``cluster_mass``, where ``cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical_origin)`` (plan §148 — credibility
5: is disclosed separately, NOT a mass factor) and every derivative copy contributes ZERO. This is the executable form of plan
6: §148: 50 copies of one press release count ONCE, at the origin's authority — the vax-defense.
7: 
8: POSTURE (binding):
9:   * ADVISORY ONLY. Weight-mass is a DISCLOSED side-output. The 4-role D8 release policy
10:     (``roles/release_policy.py``) stays the single binding release gate; ``strict_verify``'s six
11:     checks stay the only binding faithfulness gate. This module touches neither.
12:   * DEFAULT-OFF byte-identical: ``PG_SWEEP_WEIGHT_MASS`` (no production caller; pure library).
13:   * Copies contribute ZERO to the mass; adding a copy of ANY authority cannot inflate the mass
14:     (origin-cluster invariant — copies are excluded, the canonical origin alone carries the mass).
15:   * PURE: no row mutation, no network, no faithfulness-file import; LAW VI env-overridable; snake_case.
16: 
17: This issue ships ONLY the pure aggregator. Removing the journal count-floor, wiring weight-mass into
18: ``corpus_adequacy_gate``, and the per-claim clinical source-type veto are the gate-touching follow-up
19: (I-cred-006b) with their own flag — they modify faithfulness-adjacent gates.
20: 
21: INPUT join (all on the stable per-evidence ``evidence_id``):
22:   * ``rows``: evidence rows, each carrying ``evidence_id``, ``origin_cluster_id`` + ``is_canonical_origin``
23:     (Phase-4 assignment merged onto the row by the caller), and ``authority_score``. A row with no
24:     ``origin_cluster_id`` is treated as its OWN independent origin (it was never flagged a copy).
25:   * ``claims``: Phase-5 atomic claims (``claim_cluster_id`` + ``evidence_id``).
26:   * ``judgments``: Phase-2 credibility judgments (``evidence_id`` -> ``credibility_weight``); a canonical
27:     with NO judgment uses ``credibility_weight = 1.0`` (mass = pure authority).
28: """
29: from __future__ import annotations
30: 
31: import math
32: import os
33: from dataclasses import dataclass
34: from typing import Any
35: 
36: _FLAG = "PG_SWEEP_WEIGHT_MASS"
37: _OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
38: 
39: 
40: def weight_mass_enabled() -> bool:
41:     """True unless ``PG_SWEEP_WEIGHT_MASS`` is unset/falsey (default OFF => byte-identical)."""
42:     return os.environ.get(_FLAG, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
43: 
44: 
45: def _num(value: Any) -> float:
46:     """Coerce to a finite float; non-numeric / NaN / inf -> 0.0 (fail-soft on a disclosure signal)."""
47:     try:
48:         x = float(value)
49:     except (TypeError, ValueError):
50:         return 0.0
51:     return 0.0 if (math.isnan(x) or math.isinf(x)) else x
52: 
53: 
54: def _clamp01(value: Any) -> float:
55:     x = _num(value)
56:     return 0.0 if x < 0.0 else 1.0 if x > 1.0 else x
57: 
58: 
59: @dataclass
60: class OriginContribution:
61:     """One independent origin's contribution to a claim cluster's weight-mass."""
62: 
63:     origin_cluster_id: str
64:     canonical_evidence_id: str
65:     authority_score: float       # of the canonical origin
66:     credibility_weight: float    # Phase-2 weight of the canonical origin (1.0 if none)
67:     cluster_mass: float          # = authority_score(canonical) ONLY (plan §148); credibility disclosed, not a factor
68:     copy_count: int              # derivative copies attributed to this origin (disclosure)
69: 
70: 
71: @dataclass
72: class ClaimWeightMass:
73:     """A claim cluster's aggregated, copy-uninflatable weight-mass + its origin breakdown."""
74: 
75:     claim_cluster_id: str
76:     weight_mass: float
77:     independent_origin_count: int
78:     contributions: list
79: 
80: 
81: def _origin_id_for_row(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
82:     ocid = str(row.get("origin_cluster_id", "") or "").strip()
83:     # An uncollapsed row (never flagged a copy) is its OWN independent origin.
84:     return ocid if ocid else f"origin::{row.get('evidence_id', '')}"
85: 
86: 
87: def aggregate_weight_mass(
88:     claims: list,
89:     rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
90:     judgments: list,
91: ) -> list[ClaimWeightMass]:
92:     """Aggregate per-claim-cluster origin-cluster weight-mass — ADVISORY, pure, no row mutation.
93: 
94:     For each claim cluster, group its supporting rows by ``origin_cluster_id``; for each origin use the
95:     CANONICAL origin's ``authority_score`` as the cluster mass (credibility is disclosed, not a factor —
96:     plan §148); copies contribute
97:     ZERO (only ``copy_count`` for disclosure). The claim weight-mass is the sum once per origin cluster.
98:     """
99:     rows = list(rows or [])
100:     row_by_eid = {str(r.get("evidence_id", "")): r for r in rows}
101:     cred_by_eid: dict[str, float] = {}
102:     for judgment in (judgments or []):
103:         eid = str(getattr(judgment, "evidence_id", "") or "")
104:         if eid:
105:             cred_by_eid[eid] = _clamp01(getattr(judgment, "credibility_weight", None))
106: 
107:     # The canonical row per origin cluster (global across all rows), so a claim supported only by
108:     # COPIES still attributes the mass to the origin's authority, never a copy's. VALIDATED: each
109:     # COLLAPSED origin must carry EXACTLY ONE is_canonical_origin row (Phase-4 metadata) — FAIL-LOUD
110:     # on missing/duplicate, never fail-soft to a member (a copy must never become the mass carrier;
111:     # Codex #1155 P1-1). An uncollapsed row is its own singleton canonical.
112:     canonical_by_origin: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
113:     canonical_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
114:     collapsed_origins: set[str] = set()
115:     for row in rows:
116:         ocid = _origin_id_for_row(row)
117:         if str(row.get("origin_cluster_id", "") or "").strip():
118:             collapsed_origins.add(ocid)
119:             if row.get("is_canonical_origin"):
120:                 canonical_counts[ocid] = canonical_counts.get(ocid, 0) + 1
121:                 canonical_by_origin[ocid] = row
122:         else:
123:             canonical_by_origin.setdefault(ocid, row)  # uncollapsed singleton = its own canonical
124:     for ocid in sorted(collapsed_origins):
125:         count = canonical_counts.get(ocid, 0)
126:         if count != 1:
127:             raise ValueError(
128:                 f"weight_mass: origin cluster {ocid!r} must carry EXACTLY ONE is_canonical_origin "
129:                 f"row (Phase-4 canonical metadata); found {count}. No fail-soft fallback — a copy "
130:                 f"must never become the mass carrier (Codex #1155 P1-1)."
131:             )
132: 
133:     claim_eids: dict[str, list] = {}
134:     for claim in (claims or []):
135:         ccid = str(getattr(claim, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
136:         eid = str(getattr(claim, "evidence_id", "") or "")
137:         if ccid:
138:             claim_eids.setdefault(ccid, []).append(eid)
139: 
140:     out: list[ClaimWeightMass] = []
141:     for ccid in sorted(claim_eids):
142:         members_by_origin: dict[str, set] = {}
143:         for eid in claim_eids[ccid]:
144:             row = row_by_eid.get(eid)
145:             if row is None:
146:                 continue
147:             members_by_origin.setdefault(_origin_id_for_row(row), set()).add(eid)
148: 
149:         contributions: list[OriginContribution] = []
150:         for ocid in sorted(members_by_origin):
151:             members = members_by_origin[ocid]
152:             canonical = canonical_by_origin[ocid]  # guaranteed present by the validation above
153:             canon_eid = str(canonical.get("evidence_id", ""))
154:             authority = _clamp01(canonical.get("authority_score"))
155:             credibility = cred_by_eid.get(canon_eid, 1.0)  # no judgment => neutral 1.0
156:             contributions.append(OriginContribution(
157:                 origin_cluster_id=ocid,
158:                 canonical_evidence_id=canon_eid,
159:                 authority_score=authority,
160:                 credibility_weight=credibility,
161:                 # cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical origin) ONLY (plan §148). credibility_weight
162:                 # is carried as a DISCLOSED field but is NOT a mass factor: folding it in breaks the
163:                 # no-inflation invariant (a high-authority / low-credibility origin, 0.8x0.1=0.08, could
164:                 # be OVERTAKEN by adding a lower-authority copy with no judgment, 0.3x1.0=0.3 — adding a
165:                 # copy raises the mass). Mass is pure independence-authority; credibility is disclosed
166:                 # separately and combined downstream (Phase 7/8). Codex #1155 iter-2 P1.
167:                 cluster_mass=authority,
168:                 # Supporting members that are NOT the canonical = the derivative copies of this
169:                 # origin backing this claim. Counting "members minus canonical" (not len-1) is
170:                 # correct even when the claim is supported ONLY by copies and the canonical row
171:                 # backs a different claim (Codex #1155 P2-2 — no 0-copy undercount).
172:                 copy_count=sum(1 for m in members if m != canon_eid),
173:             ))
174: 
175:         out.append(ClaimWeightMass(
176:             claim_cluster_id=ccid,
177:             weight_mass=sum(c.cluster_mass for c in contributions),
178:             independent_origin_count=len(contributions),
179:             contributions=contributions,
180:         ))
181:     return out

75:     high_cred = _float_env(_ENV_HIGH_CRED, _DEFAULT_HIGH_CRED)
76:     low_cred = _float_env(_ENV_LOW_CRED, _DEFAULT_LOW_CRED)
77:     high_min_origins = _int_env(_ENV_HIGH_MIN_ORIGINS, _DEFAULT_HIGH_MIN_ORIGINS)
78:     if origin_count >= high_min_origins and credibility >= high_cred:
79:         return "high"
80:     if origin_count < 1 or credibility < low_cred:
81:         return "low"
82:     return "moderate"
83: 
84: 
85: def populate_disclosure(
86:     verifications: list,
87:     credibility_by_evidence: dict,
88:     origin_by_evidence: dict,
89: ) -> list:
90:     """Return NEW ``SentenceVerification``s with the four disclosure fields populated — ADVISORY, pure.
91: 
92:     ``credibility_by_evidence``: ``evidence_id -> Phase-2 credibility_weight``.
93:     ``origin_by_evidence``: ``evidence_id -> Phase-4 origin_cluster_id``.
94:     Inputs are NOT mutated; ``strict_verify`` is NOT re-run; ``is_verified`` is NEVER changed.
95:     """
96:     cred_map = {str(k): v for k, v in (credibility_by_evidence or {}).items()}
97:     origin_map = {str(k): str(v) for k, v in (origin_by_evidence or {}).items()}
98: 
99:     out: list = []
100:     for sv in (verifications or []):
101:         is_verified = bool(getattr(sv, "is_verified", False))
102:         cited = _cited_evidence_ids(sv)
103:         # Distinct origin clusters among cited evidence; an unmapped evidence is its own origin.
104:         origin_count = len({origin_map.get(eid, f"singleton::{eid}") for eid in cited})
105:         # MIN credibility over cited evidence (conservative — weakest cited source); absent -> None.
106:         creds = [cred_map[eid] for eid in cited if eid in cred_map and cred_map[eid] is not None]
107:         credibility = min(creds) if creds else None
108:         out.append(dataclasses.replace(
109:             sv,
110:             span_verdict="SUPPORTS" if is_verified else "UNSUPPORTED",
111:             credibility_weight=credibility,
112:             independent_origin_count=origin_count,
113:             certainty_label=_certainty_label(is_verified, origin_count, credibility),
114:         ))
115:     return out

22: from __future__ import annotations
23: 
24: import os
25: from dataclasses import dataclass
26: from typing import Any
27: 
28: _FLAG = "PG_SWEEP_BOTHSIDES_DISCLOSURE"
29: _OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
30: 
31: 
32: def bothsides_disclosure_enabled() -> bool:
33:     """True unless ``PG_SWEEP_BOTHSIDES_DISCLOSURE`` is unset/falsey (default OFF => byte-identical)."""
34:     return os.environ.get(_FLAG, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
35: 
36: 
37: def _num(value: Any) -> float:
38:     try:
39:         x = float(value)
40:     except (TypeError, ValueError):
41:         return 0.0
42:     return 0.0 if (x != x or x in (float("inf"), float("-inf"))) else x
43: 
44: 
45: @dataclass
46: class SidePosition:
47:     """One side of a contested claim: WHAT it asserts + its transparent evidence weight."""
48: 
49:     claim_cluster_id: str
50:     subject: str
51:     predicate: str
52:     statement: str                # neutral statement of WHAT this side asserts (the claim text)
53:     weight_mass: float            # Phase-6 origin-cluster weight-mass behind this side
54:     independent_origin_count: int
55:     evidence_ids: tuple           # the cited evidence for this side (one-click span access)
56: 
57: 
58: @dataclass
59: class BothSidesBlock:
60:     """A contested topic + its 2+ positions, ordered by evidence weight (NOT by "correctness")."""
61: 
62:     subject: str
63:     sides: list
64:     source: str                   # which detector raised the contradiction (numeric/qualitative/semantic)
65:     severity: str
66: 
67: 
68: def compose_both_sides(
69:     contradiction_edges: list,
70:     weight_mass: list,
71:     claims: list,
72: ) -> list[BothSidesBlock]:
73:     """Compose one BothSidesBlock per contradiction edge — pure, no input mutation.
74: 
75:     Maps each ``ContradictionEdge``'s two ``claim_cluster_ids`` to their Phase-6 ``ClaimWeightMass``
76:     (weight + independent-origin count) and Phase-5 ``AtomicClaim`` info (subject/predicate/evidence_ids).
77:     Sides are ordered by ``weight_mass`` DESC — disclosing which side has MORE evidence weight, never
78:     asserting which is true; the low-weight side is kept, never dropped. A missing weight defaults to
79:     0.0 / 0 (fail-soft disclosure, never a crash, never a fabricated weight).
80:     """
81:     weight_by_cluster: dict[str, Any] = {}
82:     for cwm in (weight_mass or []):
83:         ccid = str(getattr(cwm, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
84:         if ccid:
85:             weight_by_cluster[ccid] = cwm
86: 
87:     info: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
88:     for claim in (claims or []):
89:         ccid = str(getattr(claim, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
90:         if not ccid:
91:             continue
92:         rec = info.setdefault(ccid, {"subject": "", "predicate": "", "statement": "", "evidence_ids": set()})
93:         if not rec["subject"]:
94:             rec["subject"] = str(getattr(claim, "subject", "") or "")
95:         if not rec["predicate"]:
96:             rec["predicate"] = str(getattr(claim, "predicate", "") or "")
97:         if not rec["statement"]:
98:             rec["statement"] = str(getattr(claim, "text", "") or "").strip()
99:         eid = str(getattr(claim, "evidence_id", "") or "")
100:         if eid:
101:             rec["evidence_ids"].add(eid)
102: 
103:     blocks: list[BothSidesBlock] = []
104:     for edge in (contradiction_edges or []):
105:         cluster_ids = tuple(getattr(edge, "claim_cluster_ids", ()) or ())
106:         if len(cluster_ids) < 2:
107:             continue  # a both-sides block needs two distinct positions
108:         sides: list[SidePosition] = []
109:         for raw_ccid in cluster_ids:
110:             ccid = str(raw_ccid)
111:             cwm = weight_by_cluster.get(ccid)
112:             rec = info.get(ccid, {})
113:             side_subject = str(rec.get("subject", "") or getattr(edge, "subject", "") or "")
114:             side_predicate = str(rec.get("predicate", "") or getattr(edge, "predicate", "") or "")
115:             # WHAT this side asserts: the claim text, falling back to subject+predicate. Without it the
116:             # disclosure cannot tell the user what Position A vs B claims (Codex #1156 P1).
117:             side_statement = (
118:                 str(rec.get("statement", "") or "").strip()
119:                 or f"{side_subject} {side_predicate}".strip()
120:             )
121:             sides.append(SidePosition(
122:                 claim_cluster_id=ccid,
123:                 subject=side_subject,
124:                 predicate=side_predicate,
125:                 statement=side_statement,
126:                 weight_mass=_num(getattr(cwm, "weight_mass", 0.0)) if cwm is not None else 0.0,
127:                 independent_origin_count=(
128:                     int(getattr(cwm, "independent_origin_count", 0) or 0) if cwm is not None else 0
129:                 ),
130:                 evidence_ids=tuple(sorted(rec.get("evidence_ids", set()))),
131:             ))
132:         # Order by weight DESC; stable claim_cluster_id tiebreak for determinism. This discloses which
133:         # side carries more independent-origin evidence weight — it does NOT assert which is correct.
134:         sides.sort(key=lambda s: (-s.weight_mass, s.claim_cluster_id))
135:         blocks.append(BothSidesBlock(
136:             subject=str(getattr(edge, "subject", "") or (sides[0].subject if sides else "")),
137:             sides=sides,
138:             source=str(getattr(edge, "source", "") or ""),
139:             severity=str(getattr(edge, "severity", "") or "review"),
140:         ))
141:     return blocks
142: 
143: 
144: def render_both_sides(blocks: list) -> str:
145:     """Neutral markdown disclosure section. Empty string for no blocks (default-OFF byte-identity).
146: 
147:     Uses only neutral framing ("the evidence diverges", "evidence weight", "independent origins",
148:     "weigh them yourself") — never a judgemental label. Shows EVERY side with its weight; the user judges.
149:     """
150:     if not blocks:
151:         return ""
152:     lines = [
153:         "## Where sources diverge",
154:         "",
155:         "On the topics below the evidence does not agree. Each position is shown with its evidence "
156:         "weight (origin-cluster weight-mass) and the number of independent origins behind it, plus its "
157:         "cited sources. Both positions are shown as they stand in the evidence; weigh them yourself.",
158:         "",
159:     ]
160:     for block in blocks:
161:         lines.append(f"### On {block.subject}, the evidence diverges")
162:         for index, side in enumerate(block.sides):
163:             label = chr(ord("A") + index)
164:             cited = ", ".join(side.evidence_ids) if side.evidence_ids else "—"
165:             statement = side.statement or "(position not stated)"
166:             lines.append(
167:                 f"- **Position {label}** — \"{statement}\" — evidence weight {side.weight_mass:.2f} "
168:                 f"across {side.independent_origin_count} independent origin(s). Cited: {cited}."
169:             )
170:         lines.append("")
171:     return "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"

6:

9: the Phase-6 ``weight_mass`` per cluster and targets the LOWEST-weight cluster's assertion (the
10: under-evidenced minority). Without that signal, generic "contrary" queries could just reinforce the
11: majority — exactly the failure this phase exists to prevent.
12:

18:     minority side; it NEVER lowers adequacy thresholds, NEVER bypasses authority / relevance scoring,

49:         return int(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)

51:         return default

53: 
54: def _coerce_weight_map(weight_by_cluster: Any) -> dict[str, float]:
55:     """Accept either a ``{claim_cluster_id: weight_mass}`` dict or a list of Phase-6 ClaimWeightMass
56:     objects, and return a normalized ``{cluster_id: float}`` map."""
57:     out: dict[str, float] = {}
58:     if isinstance(weight_by_cluster, dict):
59:         for key, value in weight_by_cluster.items():
60:             try:
61:                 out[str(key)] = float(value)
62:             except (TypeError, ValueError):
63:                 continue
64:         return out
65:     for item in (weight_by_cluster or []):
66:         cid = str(getattr(item, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
67:         if not cid:
68:             continue
69:         try:
70:             out[cid] = float(getattr(item, "weight_mass", 0.0))
71:         except (TypeError, ValueError):
72:             out[cid] = 0.0
73:     return out

75: 
76: def _minority_queries(assertion: str) -> list[str]:
77:     """Deterministic, search-friendly queries that seek MORE EVIDENCE FOR the minority assertion."""
78:     base = (assertion or "").strip()[:_int_env(_ENV_ASSERTION_CHARS, _DEFAULT_ASSERTION_CHARS)]
79:     if not base:
80:         return []
81:     return [
82:         base,                       # the minority assertion itself
83:         f"{base} evidence",
84:         f"{base} studies",
85:         f"{base} supporting research",
86:     ]

88: 
89: def build_dissent_queries(
90:     contradiction_edges: list,
91:     claims: list,
92:     weight_by_cluster: Any,
93:     *,
94:     max_queries: int | None = None,
95:     query_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], list]] = None,
96: ) -> list[str]:
97:     """Pure: emit deduped queries that seek evidence FOR the MINORITY side of each contested claim.
98: 
99:     ``weight_by_cluster``: ``{claim_cluster_id: Phase-6 weight_mass}`` (dict) or a list of ClaimWeightMass.
100:     For each edge, the minority is the cluster with the LOWEST weight_mass (ties -> the
101:     lexicographically smaller cluster_id; an UNKNOWN weight is treated as 0.0 = under-evidenced); its
102:     claim TEXT becomes the assertion to seek evidence for. Returns ``[]`` for no edges (byte-identity).
103:     An injected ``query_fn`` (minority_cluster_id, minority_assertion) -> list[str] plugs an LLM
104:     generator; a ``query_fn`` that raises falls back to the templates for that edge. NO retrieval call.
105:     """
106:     if max_queries is None:
107:         max_queries = _int_env(_ENV_MAX_QUERIES, _DEFAULT_MAX_QUERIES)
108:     if max_queries <= 0:
109:         return []  # a zero/negative cap emits NOTHING (spend/recall control — Codex #1159 diff P1)
110: 
111:     text_by_cluster: dict[str, str] = {}
112:     sp_by_cluster: dict[str, tuple] = {}
113:     for claim in (claims or []):
114:         cid = str(getattr(claim, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
115:         if not cid or cid in text_by_cluster:
116:             continue
117:         text_by_cluster[cid] = str(getattr(claim, "text", "") or "").strip()
118:         sp_by_cluster[cid] = (
119:             str(getattr(claim, "subject", "") or "").strip(),
120:             str(getattr(claim, "predicate", "") or "").strip(),
121:         )
122: 
123:     weight_map = _coerce_weight_map(weight_by_cluster)
124: 
125:     out: list[str] = []
126:     seen: set[str] = set()
127:     for edge in (contradiction_edges or []):
128:         cids = [str(c) for c in (getattr(edge, "claim_cluster_ids", ()) or ())]
129:         if len(cids) < 2:
130:             continue
131:         # Minority = lowest weight_mass; ties + unknown-weight broken deterministically by cluster_id.
132:         minority_cid = min(cids, key=lambda c: (weight_map.get(c, 0.0), c))
133:         assertion = text_by_cluster.get(minority_cid, "")
134:         if not assertion:
135:             subject, predicate = sp_by_cluster.get(minority_cid, ("", ""))
136:             assertion = " ".join(part for part in (subject, predicate) if part).strip()
137:         if query_fn is not None:
138:             try:
139:                 queries = list(query_fn(minority_cid, assertion) or [])
140:             except Exception:
141:                 queries = _minority_queries(assertion)
142:         else:
143:             queries = _minority_queries(assertion)
144:         for query in queries:
145:             normalized = str(query or "").strip()
146:             key = normalized.lower()
147:             if normalized and key not in seen:
148:                 seen.add(key)
149:                 out.append(normalized)
150:                 if len(out) >= max_queries:
151:                     return out
152:     return out
153: 
154: 
155: def build_source_stratification_plan(
156:     contested_count: int,
157:     available_backends: list,
158:     *,
159:     per_type_quota: dict | None = None,
160: ) -> dict:
161:     """Pure: an ADVISORY per-source-type quota hint so dissent retrieval is stratified across web /
162:     academic / open-access / regulatory — never a hard override that could STARVE the consensus side.
163: 
164:     Empty plan for ``contested_count <= 0`` or no backends (byte-identity). When ``per_type_quota`` is
165:     given it is used (filtered to positive quotas on listed backends); otherwise a small even per-backend
166:     floor is emitted so each available source type gets some dissent budget.
167:     """
168:     if contested_count <= 0:
169:         return {}
170:     backends = [str(b).strip() for b in (available_backends or []) if str(b).strip()]
171:     if not backends:
172:         return {}
173:     if per_type_quota:
174:         plan = {b: int(per_type_quota.get(b, 0)) for b in backends}
175:         return {b: q for b, q in plan.items() if q > 0}
176:     per_backend = max(1, _int_env(_ENV_PER_BACKEND, _DEFAULT_PER_BACKEND))
177:     return {b: per_backend for b in backends}

56: 
57: # --- native contract keys (read from the scope template ONLY) ----------------------------
58: _KEY_REPORT_CONTRACT = "per_query_report_contract"
59: _KEY_REQUIRED_ENTITIES = "required_entities"
60: _KEY_ENTITY_ID = "id"
61: _KEY_ENTITY_SEVERITY = "severity"
62: _KEY_ENTITY_S0_CATEGORY = "s0_category"
63: _KEY_ENTITY_CONTENT_REQS = "coverage_content_requirements"
64: # Canonical-identifier match pairs: (entity_key, record_key). The native scope entity
65: # declares its canonical URL under `url_pattern` (clinical.yaml regulatory entities) and its
66: # trial identity under `doi`/`pmid`; the resolved evidence RECORD carries the served `url`
67: # (M3b normalizes `source_url` -> `url`) plus `doi`/`pmid`. The match is EXACT EQUALITY per
68: # pair (P2 #4): a broad/fragment `url_pattern` can never `==` a full resolved URL, so it
69: # fails closed. The pairing is intentionally ASYMMETRIC (entity `url_pattern` vs record `url`).
70: _CANONICAL_MATCH_PAIRS = (("doi", "doi"), ("pmid", "pmid"), ("url_pattern", "url"))
71: # The entity-side keys the builder recognizes as a canonical identifier (used by the
72: # fail-loud "entity must declare a recognized identifier" guard).
73: _ENTITY_CANONICAL_KEYS = tuple(entity_key for entity_key, _ in _CANONICAL_MATCH_PAIRS)
74: _RECORD_TEXT_KEY = "text"
75: 
76: _WHITESPACE_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
77: _CLAIM_HASH_HEX_LEN = 8
78: 
79: # --- evidence-record normalization (M3b; LOAD-BEARING, deterministic, NO network) ---------
80: # The raw evidence_pool.json row carries `source_url` + the evidence text under `direct_quote`
81: # (the field strict_verify's ProvenanceToken spans index into — see provenance_generator.py)
82: # with `statement` as a fallback. It carries NO doi/pmid/url keys. M3b NORMALIZES each row into
83: # the builder's `{text, doi?, pmid?, url?}` record contract so coverage can match the entity's
84: # canonical identifiers. EXACT-equality coverage (P2 #4) means the DOI/PMID must be the bare
85: # canonical token (e.g. `10.1056/NEJMoa2107519`, `34170647`) — never a URL-embedded fragment.
86: _RAW_TEXT_KEYS = ("direct_quote", "statement", "text")
87: _RAW_SOURCE_URL_KEYS = ("source_url", "url")
88: # Deterministic DOI: the canonical `10.<registrant>/<suffix>` form (CrossRef pattern).
89: _DOI_RE = re.compile(r"10\.\d{4,9}/[-._;()/:A-Za-z0-9]+")
90: # Publisher URL path-suffixes appended AFTER the DOI in a landing-page URL (e.g.
91: # frontiersin.org/.../10.3389/fphar.2022.1016639/full). Trimmed so the extracted DOI is the
92: # bare canonical token that EXACTLY equals the entity's `doi` (P2 #4). Order matters: longest
93: # first. Trailing punctuation (a `.` or `;` lifted from prose) is also stripped.
94: _DOI_URL_SUFFIXES = ("/full", "/abstract", "/pdf", "/meta", "/html", "/epdf")
95: _DOI_TRAILING_PUNCT = ".,;)"
96: # Deterministic PMID: a PubMed URL path id (`pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/<id>` or `/pubmed/<id>`)
97: # or an explicit `PMID: <id>` token. Bare numeric so it `==` the entity's `pmid` (int -> str).
98: _PMID_URL_RE = re.compile(r"(?:ncbi\.nlm\.nih\.gov/(?:pubmed|m/pubmed)?/?|/pubmed/)(\d+)")
99: _PMID_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\bPMID:?\s*(\d+)", re.IGNORECASE)
100: 
101: 
102: @dataclass
103: class NativeGateBBundle:
104:     """Builder output (Codex P2 #2): the sweep-ready inputs plus an audit map.
105: 
106:     The builder does NO file I/O — it RETURNS this bundle. M3b writes
107:     `four_role_claim_audit.json` from `audit_map` next to the run. `inputs` is fed
108:     verbatim to `run_four_role_evaluation`.
109:     """
110: 
111:     inputs: FourRoleEvaluationInputs
112:     audit_map: dict[str, dict]
113: 
114: 
115: def _normalize_sentence(sentence: str) -> str:
116:     """Lowercase + collapse whitespace (the basis for the deterministic claim_id hash)."""
117:     return _WHITESPACE_RE.sub(" ", sentence.lower()).strip()
118: 
119: 
120: def _row_text(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
121:     """The evidence text the strict_verify spans were validated against (first non-empty).
122: 
123:     Prefers `direct_quote` (the field ProvenanceToken char-spans index into), then `statement`,
124:     then `text`. Empty text is NOT raised here — the builder's `_resolve_evidence` fails closed
125:     on empty text at claim-resolution time (so an evidence row never cited stays harmless).
126:     """
127:     for key in _RAW_TEXT_KEYS:
128:         value = row.get(key)
129:         if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip():
130:             return value.strip()
131:     return ""
132: 
133: 
134: def _row_source_url(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
135:     """The record's full canonical source URL (verbatim), from `source_url` then `url`."""
136:     for key in _RAW_SOURCE_URL_KEYS:
137:         value = row.get(key)
138:         if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip():
139:             return value.strip()
140:     return ""
141: 
142: 
143: def _extract_doi(*texts: str) -> str | None:
144:     """Deterministic bare-DOI extraction (`10.<reg>/<suffix>`) from the given strings.
145: 
146:     The greedy DOI body can absorb a publisher landing-page suffix (`/full`, `/pdf`, ...) or a
147:     trailing punctuation mark lifted from prose. Both are deterministically trimmed so the
148:     returned DOI is the bare canonical token that EXACTLY equals the entity's `doi` (P2 #4);
149:     coverage stays fail-closed (a mis-trim simply fails to match — never over-credits).
150:     """
151:     for text in texts:
152:         if not text:
153:             continue
154:         match = _DOI_RE.search(text)
155:         if not match:
156:             continue
157:         doi = match.group(0)
158:         for suffix in _DOI_URL_SUFFIXES:
159:             if doi.endswith(suffix):
160:                 doi = doi[: -len(suffix)]
161:                 break
162:         return doi.rstrip(_DOI_TRAILING_PUNCT)
163:     return None
164: 
165: 
166: def _extract_pmid(*texts: str) -> str | None:
167:     """Deterministic bare-PMID extraction from a PubMed URL or an explicit `PMID:` token."""
168:     for text in texts:
169:         if not text:
170:             continue
171:         url_match = _PMID_URL_RE.search(text)
172:         if url_match:
173:             return url_match.group(1)
174:         token_match = _PMID_TOKEN_RE.search(text)
175:         if token_match:
176:             return token_match.group(1)
177:     return None
178: 
179: 
180: def normalize_evidence_pool_lookup(
181:     ev_pool: Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]],
182: ) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
183:     """Build the builder's `evidence_id -> {text, doi?, pmid?, url?}` lookup from a raw pool.
184: 
185:     DETERMINISTIC, NO NETWORK (M3b). Keys are preserved verbatim so they match the
186:     ProvenanceToken.evidence_id space (the same `ev_pool` keyspace the generator used). For each
187:     row: `url` = the record's `source_url` (full canonical URL, verbatim); `doi` = a bare DOI
188:     extracted by regex from the source_url then text (absent if none); `pmid` = a bare PMID from
189:     a PubMed URL or explicit token (absent if none); `text` = the evidence text strict_verify
190:     validated against (`direct_quote`/`statement`/`text`). Optional identifiers are only added
191:     when present so an absent identifier is genuinely absent (the builder treats `None`/`""` as
192:     no-match, fail-closed). Never reads `outputs/dr_benchmark/`.
193:     """
194:     lookup: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
195:     for evidence_id, row in ev_pool.items():
196:         if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
197:             raise ValueError(
198:                 f"normalize_evidence_pool_lookup: evidence row {evidence_id!r} is not a mapping "
199:                 f"({type(row).__name__}); cannot normalize a non-record (fail-closed)."
200:             )
201:         text = _row_text(row)
202:         url = _row_source_url(row)
203:         record: dict[str, Any] = {_RECORD_TEXT_KEY: text}
204:         if url:
205:             record["url"] = url
206:         doi = _extract_doi(url, text)
207:         if doi is not None:
208:             record["doi"] = doi
209:         pmid = _extract_pmid(url, text)
210:         if pmid is not None:
211:             record["pmid"] = pmid
212:         lookup[evidence_id] = record
213:     return lookup
214: 
215:

217:     """Return the NATIVE required entities for `slug`; fail closed if absent/empty.
218:

220:     (never a vacuous empty-denominator pass).

618:     # PT11 — every numeric claim has a citation token.
619:     # Only check the PROSE portion of the report (everything before the
620:     # "## Methods" heading). The methods and bibliography sections
621:     # contain protocol numbers (tier bounds like "30-60%", retrieval
622:     # dates, model names with version numbers) that are specifications,
623:     # not empirical claims needing citations.
624:     # I-gen-003 (2026-05-14): also stop at the "Limitations" block. Like
625:     # the Methods specs excluded above, the Limitations section is
626:     # POLARIS-generated meta-prose — corpus-skew percentages, the
627:     # contradiction-detector's own relative-difference telemetry
628:     # (e.g. "relative difference 135.7%"), completeness-gap counts. These
629:     # are NOT empirical claims from sources and must not be PT11-scored.
630:     # Without this, POLARIS's own telemetry decimals false-fail PT11
631:     # (V4 Pro smoke #3: 2 of 3 "uncited" decimals were Limitations
632:     # telemetry, tripping the gate to abort_evaluator_critical). Match
633:     # the heading at either "## " or "### " level, case-insensitively.
634:     _cut_idxs: list[int] = []
635:     methods_idx = report_text.lower().find("\n## methods")
636:     if methods_idx > 0:
637:         _cut_idxs.append(methods_idx)
638:     _limitations_match = re.search(
639:         r"\n#{2,3} +limitations\b", report_text, re.IGNORECASE
640:     )
641:     if _limitations_match:
642:         _cut_idxs.append(_limitations_match.start())
643:     if _cut_idxs:
644:         prose_only = report_text[:min(_cut_idxs)]
645:     else:
646:         prose_only = report_text
647:     text_stripped = re.sub(r"\[#ev:[^\]]+\]", "", prose_only)
648:     # Only check DECIMAL numbers (the empirical claims) — integers like
649:     # study-ID "STEP 1" or "week 68" are study markers, not claims.
650:     #
651:     # Look-ahead is to the end of the current sentence (up to `.!?`),
652:     # not a fixed 80-char window — decimals early in a long sentence
653:     # should get credit for a citation attached at the sentence end.
654:     #
655:     # M-30 (2026-04-20): use abbreviation-aware sentence boundary
656:     # detection. `vs.`, `e.g.`, `etc.`, `Fig.`, etc. must NOT split a
657:     # sentence — otherwise "10.7% vs. 4.8%, 8.1% vs. 2.7% ... [7]"
658:     # is incorrectly scored as having uncited decimals.
659:     numeric_matches = list(re.finditer(
660:         r"(?<![A-Za-z0-9.])(-?\d+\.\d+)",
661:         text_stripped,
662:     ))
663:     uncited = 0
664:     # M-34 (2026-04-21): widen lookahead so the abbreviation-aware
665:     # sentence-end helper can actually find the terminator. Long
666:     # regulatory/clinical sentences (300-450 chars) are legitimate —
667:     # V23 had three sentences where the citation was at char 350-450
668:     # but the helper only saw the first 200 chars, returned None, and
669:     # fell back to 150. Slice now caps at 1000 chars; None-fallback
670:     # returns the full slice instead of narrowing to 150.
671:     for m in numeric_matches:
672:         after_text = text_stripped[m.end():m.end() + 1000]
673:         lookahead_end = _next_real_sentence_end(after_text)
674:         if lookahead_end is None:
675:             lookahead_end = len(after_text)
676:         snippet_after = after_text[:lookahead_end]
677:         if not re.search(r"\[\d+\]|\[#ev:", snippet_after):
678:             # Fallback: allow markers BEFORE the number. Walk back to
679:             # the previous real sentence boundary (abbreviation-aware).
680:             # Same widening applies to the back-lookup.
681:             back_text = text_stripped[max(0, m.start() - 1000):m.start()]
682:             prev_end = _prev_real_sentence_end(back_text)
683:             snippet_before = back_text[prev_end + 1:] if prev_end >= 0 else back_text
684:             if not re.search(r"\[\d+\]|\[#ev:", snippet_before):
685:                 uncited += 1
686:     pt11 = uncited < max(3, len(numeric_matches) // 10)  # allow <=10% uncited
687:     results.append(RuleCheckResult(
688:         "PT11", "Numeric claims have citation markers", pt11,
689:         f"{uncited} numeric claims without adjacent citation marker "
690:         f"(out of {len(numeric_matches)} decimals in prose)."
691:         if not pt11 else "",
692:     ))
693: 
694:     # PT12 — no citations on unverified sentences
695:     # Heuristic: find sentences containing [n] markers, check that the
696:     # evidence pool has at least one entry referenced. We can't do full
697:     # provenance verification without the token data, but we can check
698:     # that markers [1]..[N] don't exceed the bibliography size.
699:     # M-5 (Codex pass 5): scan only the pre-bibliography portion.
700:     # Bibliography entries legitimately include bracketed years in
701:     # source titles (e.g., "Best Guide on RAG Pipeline [2025]") that
702:     # must not be misread as citation markers into the evidence pool.
703:     biblio_idx = report_text.lower().find("\n## bibliography")
704:     prose_for_markers = (
705:         report_text[:biblio_idx] if biblio_idx > 0 else report_text
706:     )
707:     max_marker = 0
708:     for m in re.finditer(r"\[(\d+)\]", prose_for_markers):
709:         try:
710:             n = int(m.group(1))
711:             if n > max_marker:
712:                 max_marker = n
713:         except ValueError:
714:             continue
715:     pt12 = max_marker <= len(evidence_pool) if evidence_pool else True
716:     results.append(RuleCheckResult(
717:         "PT12", "Citation markers don't exceed bibliography size", pt12,
718:         f"max_marker={max_marker} but evidence_pool has {len(evidence_pool)}."
719:         if not pt12 else "",
720:     ))
721: 
722:     # PT13 — Gap-2: hedging on superlative / comparative claims.
723:     # Scans the prose portion of the report for unhedged superlatives
724:     # ("largest", "best", "better than X") that are NOT anchored to a
725:     # source verb ("reported", "described as", "one review found").

658: 
659: def apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate() -> None:
660:     """Make the full-capability slate AUTHORITATIVE over .env / conservative defaults (FLOOR semantics).
661: 
662:     Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-1: ``setdefault`` was WRONG. ``load_dotenv`` (openrouter_client import)
663:     puts .env values into ``os.environ`` BEFORE this slate runs, so a conservative .env default
664:     (e.g. ``PG_MOST_MAX_EVIDENCE=300``, ``PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND=6``) SURVIVED the setdefault and
665:     silently throttled the benchmark below the slate. Fix: every numeric key is a **FLOOR** —
666:     ``max(existing, slate)`` — so a HIGHER operator/.env value (more capability, e.g.
667:     ``PG_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=600`` in .env) is KEPT, but a LOWER default is RAISED to full capability.
668:     No silent downgrade in either direction (operator no-downgrade directive). Feature flags in
669:     ``_BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS`` are FORCED ON (a benchmark feature off is a capability downgrade).
670:     Exact-value flags in ``_BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS`` are forced to their slate value.
671: 
672:     Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-2: MUST run BEFORE the sweep / live_retriever / state imports, which cache
673:     ``PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX`` / ``PG_LIVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT`` / ``PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND`` as import-time module
674:     constants. ``main()`` applies it before ``load_locked_questions()``; ``run_gate_b_query`` applies it
675:     before the sweep import. ``preflight_full_capability`` then validates the LIVE module constants so a
676:     too-late application is caught fail-closed.
677:     """
678:     for name, value in _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE.items():
679:         if name in _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS or name in _BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS:
680:             os.environ[name] = value                       # force exact value — no silent benchmark drift
681:             continue
682:         try:
683:             current = float(os.environ.get(name, value))
684:         except (TypeError, ValueError):
685:             current = float(value)
686:         os.environ[name] = str(int(max(current, float(value))))   # FLOOR: raise-to-slate, keep-if-higher
687:     # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN is an import-time constant in openrouter_client (cached before this slate via
688:     # the role import chain), so the floor above only fixes the env; sync the live module global too so
689:     # check_run_budget enforces the floored cap and run_one_query's manifest reads it (Codex iter-1 P1-2).
690:     from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import set_max_cost_per_run
691:     set_max_cost_per_run(float(os.environ["PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN"]))
692: 
693: 
694: def preflight_full_capability() -> None:
695:     """FAIL CLOSED if the effective benchmark config is below full capability or unobservable — so a
696:     silent throttle (the ~40-URL bug) can NEVER reach a paid run undetected. Raises RuntimeError."""
697:     for name, floor in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_FLOORS.items():
698:         # mirror run_one_query's read of the real PG_SWEEP_* knob (defaults 12/12/40 if absent).
699:         _defaults = {"PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP": "40", "PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER": "12", "PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2": "12"}
700:         try:
701:             eff = int(os.getenv(name, _defaults.get(name, "0")))
702:         except ValueError:
703:             raise RuntimeError(f"benchmark preflight: {name}={os.getenv(name)!r} is not an int")
704:         if eff < floor:
705:             raise RuntimeError(
706:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {name}={eff} < full-capability floor {floor} — the run "
707:                 f"would be SILENTLY THROTTLED (the ~40-URL bug). Set {name}>={floor} or fix the slate."
708:             )
709:     for flag in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS:
710:         if os.getenv(flag, "0").strip() not in ("1", "true", "True"):
711:             raise RuntimeError(
712:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {flag} is not enabled — the feature is dead-by-config "
713:                 f"or its firing is unobservable (tracker off). Enable it before the run."
714:             )
715:     # I-ready-017 FX-03 (#1107) Codex iter-2 P1: the cited-span window must stay BOUNDED. An oversized
716:     # PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES makes _cited_window_text expand to the whole direct_quote — BUG-02
717:     # whole-doc evidence WITH the cited-span flag on. The slate force-exacts it to the 400-byte policy;
718:     # this fails closed if it is ever outside (0, ceiling] (force-exact removed / slate value changed).
719:     if os.getenv("PG_GATE_B_CITED_SPAN", "0").strip() in ("1", "true", "True"):
720:         try:
721:             _win = int(os.getenv("PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES", "400"))
722:         except ValueError:
723:             raise RuntimeError(
724:                 "benchmark preflight FAILED: PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES="
725:                 f"{os.getenv('PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES')!r} is not an int."

 succeeded in 7094ms:

119:     # ── 1. Deterministic release-blocking rule failures ──

123:         if rule.passed:

136:                 reasons.append(code)

149:             code = RELEASE_BLOCKING_RULES["PT08"]

161:         reasons.append("judge_skipped_d8_binding")
162:     elif judge_result is None:
163:         judge_parse_ok = False
164:         reasons.append("judge_parse_failed")
165:     elif not getattr(judge_result, "parse_ok", False):
166:         judge_parse_ok = False
167:         reasons.append("judge_parse_failed")
168:     else:
169:         verdicts = getattr(judge_result, "verdicts", {}) or {}

179:         if "citation_tightness" in needs:

196:             comp_thin = (

199:                 and getattr(completeness, "covered_fraction", 1.0) < 0.5
200:             )
201:             if comp_thin:

204:                     reasons.append("judge_completeness_needs_revision")

206:         if len(needs) >= 3:
207:             if "judge_multi_axis_needs_revision" not in reasons:
208:                 reasons.append("judge_multi_axis_needs_revision")

 succeeded in 3385ms:
151: def saturation_decision(
152:     *,
153:     verdict: str,
154:     round_index: int,
155:     max_rounds: int,
156:     novelty: float,
157:     eps: float,
158: ) -> str:
159:     """Decide the next saturation action (brief §2.1). PRIORITY LADDER — order
160:     matters; budget is checked BEFORE novelty.
161: 
162:     Returns one of {CONTINUE, STOP_SUFFICIENT, STOP_NOVELTY, STOP_BUDGET}.
163: 
164:     - verdict == proceed -> STOP_SUFFICIENT (gap closed).
165:     - verdict == abort -> STOP_BUDGET (the Phase-3 gate returns `abort` when
166:       `round_index >= max_rounds`; an explicit terminal, never unhandled).
167:     - verdict == expand AND round_index+1 >= max_rounds -> STOP_BUDGET (rounds
168:       exhausted).
169:     - verdict == expand AND round_index >= 1 AND novelty < eps -> STOP_NOVELTY
170:       (the last round added < eps novel rows — the curve flattened).
171:     - else (expand, rounds left, novelty >= eps) -> CONTINUE.
172:     """
173:     if verdict == "proceed":
174:         return STOP_SUFFICIENT
175:     # abort is a terminal verdict (rounds/budget exhausted at the gate).
176:     if verdict == "abort":
177:         return STOP_BUDGET
178:     # From here: verdict == expand (or any non-proceed/non-abort -> treat as
179:     # expand-style; budget-bounded). Budget BEFORE novelty.
180:     if round_index + 1 >= max_rounds:
181:         return STOP_BUDGET
182:     if round_index >= 1 and novelty < eps:
183:         return STOP_NOVELTY
184:     return CONTINUE
185: 
186: 
187: # ── budget accounting (PRE-SPEND, worst-case) ────────────────────────────────
188: 
189: def per_query_discovery_cost(adapter_count: int) -> int:
190:     """Worst-case DISCOVERY calls per gap query (brief §2.2, iter-5 P1 #1).
191: 
192:     core Serper + core S2 (`live_retriever.py:1790`,`:1806`) = 2 calls/query,
193:     PLUS the need-type dispatcher which calls EACH routed adapter inside
194:     `for q in queries` (`domain_backends.py:660`) = `adapter_count` calls PER gap
195:     query. (Fetch + OpenAlex are bounded by `fetch_cap`, not multiplied per gap
196:     query — out of this cap's scope.)
197:     """
198:     return 2 + max(0, int(adapter_count))
199: 
200: 
201: @dataclass
202: class BudgetPreflight:
203:     """Result of the PRE-SPEND budget preflight for one expansion round."""
204: 
205:     allowed_queries: list[str]
206:     fired_cost: int            # worst-case discovery calls this round will spend
207:     truncated: bool            # True iff gap queries were truncated to fit
208:     exhausted: bool            # True iff remaining budget cannot fund any query
209: 
210: 
211: def preflight_round_budget(
212:     *,
213:     gap_queries: list[str],
214:     cumulative_discovery_calls: int,
215:     max_discovery_calls: int,
216:     cost_per_query: int,
217: ) -> BudgetPreflight:
218:     """PRE-SPEND truncation so a round's WORST-CASE discovery spend CANNOT push
219:     `cumulative_discovery_calls` over `max_discovery_calls` (brief §2.2, iter-3
220:     P1; INVARIANT P4-14).
221: 
222:     `remaining = MAX - cumulative`; if `remaining <= 0` -> exhausted (no query may
223:     fire). Else the round may fire at most `floor(remaining / cost_per_query)` gap
224:     queries — TRUNCATE `gap_queries` to that many.
225:     """
226:     cost = max(1, int(cost_per_query))
227:     remaining = int(max_discovery_calls) - int(cumulative_discovery_calls)
228:     if remaining <= 0:
229:         return BudgetPreflight(
230:             allowed_queries=[], fired_cost=0, truncated=bool(gap_queries),
231:             exhausted=True,
232:         )
233:     max_fire = remaining // cost
234:     if max_fire <= 0:
235:         # Remaining budget cannot fund even one gap query at worst-case cost.
236:         return BudgetPreflight(
237:             allowed_queries=[], fired_cost=0, truncated=bool(gap_queries),
238:             exhausted=True,
239:         )
240:     allowed = list(gap_queries[:max_fire])
241:     return BudgetPreflight(
242:         allowed_queries=allowed,
243:         fired_cost=len(allowed) * cost,
244:         truncated=len(allowed) < len(gap_queries),
245:         exhausted=False,
246:     )
247: 
248: 

 succeeded in 5646ms:
249: # ── the injectable saturation orchestrator ───────────────────────────────────
250: 
251: @dataclass
252: class RoundOutcome:
253:     """One saturation round's outputs from the injected `run_round_fn`.
254: 
255:     The orchestrator stays client-free: the runner supplies a closure that does
256:     the actual retrieval -> select -> [V30/upload inject] -> gate; the smoke
257:     supplies a capture-only stub with controlled per-round evidence.
258:     """
259: 
260:     # Cumulative RETRIEVED corpus rows (for the novelty metric; `source_url`).
261:     cumulative_retrieved_rows: list[Any]
262:     # The BILLED generator-visible set Phase 3 certifies (post-select+V30+upload).
263:     evidence_for_gen: list[Any]
264:     # The PlanSufficiencyReport for THIS round's billed set.
265:     sufficiency_report: Any
266:     # The RAW rows THIS round RETRIEVED (the novelty DENOMINATOR) -- INCLUDES
267:     # canonical-URL duplicates already present in the corpus, so the novelty
268:     # fraction can fall below 1.0 as later rounds re-fetch the same sources.
269:     # NOT the deduped additions: handing the deduped set here makes novelty
270:     # degenerate (always 1.0 unless a round adds exactly zero rows), so the
271:     # `novelty < eps` flatten stop would never fire below 1.0. Round 0 = all of
272:     # round 0 (no prior corpus, so every row is novel).
273:     new_round_rows: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
274:     # The cumulative RETRIEVED corpus snapshot taken BEFORE this round (the
275:     # novelty BASELINE a round's raw rows are compared against). Round 0 =
276:     # empty (no prior corpus). Carried explicitly so the loop never has to
277:     # reconstruct "prior corpus" by object-identity subtraction (which breaks
278:     # once `new_round_rows` holds raw rows not all present in the corpus).
279:     prev_corpus_rows: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
280: 
281: 
282: @dataclass
283: class SaturationResult:
284:     """Terminal state of the saturation loop."""
285: 
286:     decision: str                 # STOP_SUFFICIENT | STOP_NOVELTY | STOP_BUDGET
287:     rounds_fired: int             # number of EXPANSION rounds fired (round 0 excl.)
288:     final_round: RoundOutcome
289:     cumulative_discovery_calls: int
290:     novelty_trajectory: list[float] = field(default_factory=list)
291:     truncated_any_round: bool = False
292: 
293: 
294: def run_saturation_loop(
295:     *,
296:     round0: RoundOutcome,
297:     run_round_fn: Callable[[list[str]], RoundOutcome],
298:     max_rounds: int,
299:     novelty_eps: float,
300:     max_discovery_calls: int,
301:     cost_per_query: int,
302:     plan: Any,
303:     log: Optional[Callable[[str], None]] = None,
304: ) -> SaturationResult:
305:     """Drive the multi-round saturation loop (brief §2.2). PURE — constructs NO
306:     HTTP client; the live retrieval round is the INJECTED `run_round_fn`.
307: 
308:     Args:
309:         round0: the already-executed round-0 outcome (today's single-pass
310:             retrieval -> select -> [V30/upload] -> gate). Round 0 is the
311:             un-truncatable baseline; its spend is NOT charged against the
312:             expansion-round budget (which starts at 0).
313:         run_round_fn: `(gap_queries) -> RoundOutcome`. The runner's closure fires
314:             a GAP-ONLY retrieval round (anchor-suppressed), merges with global
315:             evidence_id renumber, re-selects, re-injects V30/upload, re-gates.
316:         max_rounds: `PG_SATURATION_MAX_ROUNDS` — bounds total rounds (incl. 0).
317:         novelty_eps: `PG_SATURATION_NOVELTY_EPS` — epsilon for the flatten stop.
318:         max_discovery_calls: `PG_SATURATION_MAX_RETRIEVAL_CALLS` — the cumulative
319:             EXPANSION-round discovery-call cap (round 0 excluded).
320:         cost_per_query: worst-case `per_query_discovery_cost(adapter_count)`.
321:         plan: the pinned `ResearchPlan` (for `gap_sub_queries`).
322:         log: optional line logger.
323: 
324:     Returns SaturationResult with the terminal decision + the final round.
325:     """
326:     _log = log or (lambda _m: None)
327:     round_index = 0
328:     current = round0
329:     cumulative_calls = 0            # EXPANSION-round discovery spend only.
330:     novelty_trajectory: list[float] = []
331:     truncated_any = False
332: 
333:     while True:
334:         verdict = str(getattr(current.sufficiency_report, "verdict", "abort"))
335:         # Novelty of THE ROUND JUST RUN vs the cumulative corpus BEFORE it. For
336:         # round 0 there is no prior corpus, so novelty is definitionally 1.0 and
337:         # the round>=1 guard in `saturation_decision` ignores it anyway. For
338:         # expansion rounds the baseline is the round's OWN `prev_corpus_rows`
339:         # snapshot (the corpus as it stood BEFORE the round) and the denominator
340:         # is the RAW retrieved `new_round_rows` (incl. duplicates) -- so a round
341:         # that re-fetches mostly-seen sources reads a LOW novelty fraction and
342:         # the `< eps` flatten stop can actually fire. Reconstructing the prior
343:         # corpus by `cumulative - new_round_rows` would be wrong now that
344:         # `new_round_rows` holds raw rows not all present in the corpus.
345:         if round_index == 0:
346:             novelty = 1.0
347:         else:
348:             novelty = marginal_novelty(
349:                 current.prev_corpus_rows, current.new_round_rows
350:             )
351:         novelty_trajectory.append(novelty)
352: 
353:         decision = saturation_decision(
354:             verdict=verdict,
355:             round_index=round_index,
356:             max_rounds=max_rounds,
357:             novelty=novelty,
358:             eps=novelty_eps,
359:         )
360:         _log(
361:             f"[saturation]  round={round_index} verdict={verdict} "
362:             f"novelty={novelty:.3f} -> {decision}"
363:         )
364: 
365:         if decision != CONTINUE:
366:             return SaturationResult(
367:                 decision=decision,
368:                 rounds_fired=round_index,
369:                 final_round=current,
370:                 cumulative_discovery_calls=cumulative_calls,
371:                 novelty_trajectory=novelty_trajectory,
372:                 truncated_any_round=truncated_any,
373:             )
374: 
375:         # CONTINUE -> fire round N+1, GAP-ONLY. Compute the gap queries from the
376:         # CURRENT round's sufficiency report, then PRE-SPEND budget-truncate.
377:         gaps = gap_sub_queries(current.sufficiency_report, plan)
378:         if not gaps:
379:             # Defensive: a non-proceed verdict with no gap query to fire would
380:             # spin the loop. Treat as budget-terminal (nothing actionable left).
381:             _log(
382:                 "[saturation]  no gap sub-queries for under-covered "
383:                 "section(s); terminating as STOP_BUDGET"
384:             )
385:             return SaturationResult(
386:                 decision=STOP_BUDGET,
387:                 rounds_fired=round_index,
388:                 final_round=current,
389:                 cumulative_discovery_calls=cumulative_calls,
390:                 novelty_trajectory=novelty_trajectory,
391:                 truncated_any_round=truncated_any,
392:             )
393: 
394:         preflight = preflight_round_budget(
395:             gap_queries=gaps,
396:             cumulative_discovery_calls=cumulative_calls,
397:             max_discovery_calls=max_discovery_calls,
398:             cost_per_query=cost_per_query,
399:         )
400:         truncated_any = truncated_any or preflight.truncated
401:         if preflight.exhausted:
402:             _log(
403:                 "[saturation]  retrieval budget exhausted "
404:                 f"(cumulative={cumulative_calls}/{max_discovery_calls}); "
405:                 "STOP_BUDGET before firing"
406:             )
407:             return SaturationResult(
408:                 decision=STOP_BUDGET,
409:                 rounds_fired=round_index,
410:                 final_round=current,
411:                 cumulative_discovery_calls=cumulative_calls,
412:                 novelty_trajectory=novelty_trajectory,
413:                 truncated_any_round=truncated_any,
414:             )
415: 
416:         # Charge the WORST-CASE spend PRE-SPEND so the cumulative counter can
417:         # NEVER exceed MAX (INVARIANT P4-14), then fire the round.
418:         cumulative_calls += preflight.fired_cost
419:         _log(
420:             f"[saturation]  firing round {round_index + 1} with "
421:             f"{len(preflight.allowed_queries)} gap queries "
422:             f"(worst-case +{preflight.fired_cost} calls; "
423:             f"cumulative={cumulative_calls}/{max_discovery_calls})"
424:         )
425:         current = run_round_fn(preflight.allowed_queries)
426:         round_index += 1

codex
The final generator-visible rows are not just `retrieval.evidence_rows`: V30 contract rows and uploaded-document rows can be prepended after selection, and finding-dedup can rewrite the pool again immediately before generation. Any credibility sidecar keyed by `evidence_id` must be built or refreshed after those mutations if it will be rendered on verified sentences.
